sit-d94-new-year-in-funchal.title: New Year in Funchal
sit-d94-new-year-in-funchal.summary: The famous fireworks: planning where to watch (baía, miradouros), inviting people, the midnight rituals, and the first conversations of the new year.

phrase_patterns.base: Onde é que vais ver os fogos {location}?
phrase_patterns.translation: Where are you going to watch the fireworks {location}?

phrase_patterns.base: Já tens planos para a passagem de ano?
phrase_patterns.translation: Do you already have plans for New Year's Eve?

variants.text: Tens planos para o Ano Novo?
variants.translation: Do you have plans for New Year?

variants.text: Já tens programas para o Réveillon?
variants.translation: Do you already have plans for New Year's Eve?
variants.note: Réveillon is a common loanword in Portuguese.

phrase_patterns.translation: Do you want to come {activity} with us?

vocabulary.note: Refers to Funchal Bay for the fireworks.
Common in Madeira for scenic views.
Traditional to eat at midnight for good luck.
Common in Portugal, especially for colder weather.

dialogues.title: New Year's Plans with a Neighbour
dialogues.context: Maria and João, neighbours, meet on the street in Funchal a few days before New Year's Eve, discussing their plans.

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Então, João, já tens planos para a passagem de ano?
lines.translation: So, João, do you already have plans for New Year's Eve?

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Olá, Maria! Ainda não, estava a pensar em algo calmo. Talvez ir à baía, mas é sempre muita confusão.
lines.translation: Hi, Maria! Not yet, I was thinking of something calm. Maybe going to the bay, but it's always very chaotic.

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Pois é, na baía é bonito, mas é uma multidão. Nós vamos para o miradouro das Neves este ano.
lines.translation: That's true, the bay is beautiful, but it's a crowd. We're going to the Neves viewpoint this year.

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Ah, o das Neves é bom! E os fogos veem-se bem de lá?
lines.translation: Ah, Neves is good! And can you see the fireworks well from there?

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Veem-se lindamente! Queres vir connosco? Leva um agasalho, que lá em cima faz sempre um bocado de frio.
lines.translation: You see them beautifully! Do you want to come with us? Bring some warm clothing, because it's always a bit cold up there.

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Obrigado, Maria! Vou pensar nisso e depois digo-te. É uma boa ideia!
lines.translation: Thanks, Maria! I'll think about it and then tell you. It's a good idea!

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: P'ra já! Até já!
lines.translation: You're welcome! See you soon!

cultural_notes.title: New Year's Eve in Funchal
cultural_notes.body: Funchal is famous worldwide for its spectacular New Year's Eve fireworks display, officially recognized by Guinness World Records in the past. The fireworks are launched from various points around Funchal Bay, creating a stunning visual spectacle. Locals and tourists often gather along the waterfront (na baía) or at one of the many viewpoints (miradouros) around the city, like Miradouro das Neves or Miradouro da Nazaré, to get the best view. It's common for families and friends to have a special dinner before heading out to watch the show.

cultural_notes.title: Midnight Traditions
cultural_notes.body: At the stroke of midnight on New Year's Eve, several traditions are observed in Madeira, as in mainland Portugal. People typically eat twelve raisins (doze passas), one for each stroke of the clock, making a wish for each month of the new year. It's also customary to toast with sparkling wine (espumante) or champagne (champanhe) to welcome the Ano Novo. Wearing something blue for good luck is a popular belief, and some might even carry money in their hand or pocket to ensure financial prosperity.

roleplay.scenario: You're at the local mercearia (small grocery store) and a neighbour, Senhora Ana, asks about your New Year's plans and invites you somewhere.

nodes.npc_text: Olá! Já tens planos para a passagem de ano? Os fogos na baía são sempre espetaculares, não são?
nodes.npc_translation: Hello! Do you already have plans for New Year's Eve? The fireworks in the bay are always spectacular, aren't they?

options.text: Olá, Senhora Ana! Ainda não, estava a pensar em algo mais calmo.
options.translation: Hello, Senhora Ana! Not yet, I was thinking of something calmer.
options.feedback: Good start, acknowledging her and expressing a preference.

options.text: Sim, Senhora Ana. Vou ver os fogos na baía, como sempre.
options.translation: Yes, Senhora Ana. I'm going to watch the fireworks in the bay, as always.
options.feedback: Clear answer, stating your preference for the bay.

nodes.npc_text: Pois é, lá em baixo é bonito, mas é uma confusão! Nós este ano vamos para o miradouro da Nazaré. É mais calmo e vê-se tudo na mesma.
nodes.npc_translation: That's true, down there it's beautiful, but it's chaotic! This year we're going to the Nazaré viewpoint. It's calmer and you see everything just the same.

options.text: Que boa ideia! Talvez mude de planos e vá para lá também.
options.translation: What a good idea! Maybe I'll change my plans and go there too.
options.feedback: Expressing interest in her suggestion.

options.text: Obrigado, Senhora Ana, mas prefiro ficar em casa este ano.
options.translation: Thank you, Senhora Ana, but I prefer to stay home this year.
options.feedback: Politely declining the implicit invitation.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, na baía é sempre uma festa! Mas se gostas de mais sossego, o miradouro da Nazaré é uma boa opção. Queres vir connosco?
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, the bay is always a party! But if you like more quiet, the Nazaré viewpoint is a good option. Do you want to come with us?

options.text: Sim, Senhora Ana! Parece uma boa ideia. Que horas?
options.translation: Yes, Senhora Ana! It seems like a good idea. What time?
options.feedback: Accepting the invitation and asking for details.

options.text: Muito obrigado, mas já prometi ir com uns amigos para a baía.
options.translation: Thank you very much, but I already promised to go with some friends to the bay.
options.feedback: Politely declining with an explanation.

nodes.npc_text: Fantástico! Leva um agasalho, que lá em cima faz sempre um bocado de frio. Encontramo-nos lá pelas onze e meia?
nodes.npc_translation: Fantastic! Bring some warm clothing, because it's always a bit cold up there. Shall we meet there around half past eleven?

options.text: Combinado, Senhora Ana! Levo o agasalho. Até lá!
options.translation: Agreed, Senhora Ana! I'll bring the warm clothing. See you then!
options.feedback: Confirming the plan and showing enthusiasm. Roleplay ends here.

options.text: Sim, onze e meia está ótimo! Muito obrigado pelo convite.
options.translation: Yes, half past eleven is great! Thank you very much for the invitation.
options.feedback: Confirming the time and expressing gratitude. Roleplay ends here.

nodes.npc_text: Não faz mal, percebo. O importante é passar um bom Ano Novo! Passa bem!
nodes.npc_translation: No problem, I understand. The important thing is to have a good New Year! Have a good one!

options.text: Igualmente, Senhora Ana! Um feliz Ano Novo!
options.translation: You too, Senhora Ana! A happy New Year!
options.feedback: Returning the good wishes. Roleplay ends here.

options.text: Obrigado! Para si também, Senhora Ana!
options.translation: Thank you! For you too, Senhora Ana!
options.feedback: Thanking her and returning the good wishes. Roleplay ends here.

Prepare to accept or politely decline an invitation.

sit-d95-santos-populares.title: The Santos Populares
sit-d95-santos-populares.summary: The saints’ festivals in June and the village festas: Santo António, São João, São Pedro — the marchas, the sardines and espetada stalls, and joining in as a newcomer.

phrase_patterns.base: Vai aos Santos Populares este ano?
phrase_patterns.translation: Are you going to the Popular Saints' festivals this year?

variants.text: Vais aos Santos Populares este ano?
variants.translation: Are you going to the Popular Saints' festivals this year?
variants.note: Informal 'tu' form

phrase_patterns.base: Gostava de ir à festa de {local}.
phrase_patterns.translation: I'd like to go to the festival in {local}.

slots.name: local
slots.description: Name of a place or saint's festival

variants.note: Informal 'tu' form

phrase_patterns.base: É uma tradição muito {adjective} aqui na Madeira.
phrase_patterns.translation: It's a very {adjective} tradition here in Madeira.

vocabulary.note: Collective term for June festivals (Santo António, São João, São Pedro)
Common local community celebrations
Typical food during Santos Populares
Traditional Madeiran dish, often found at festivals
Iconic Madeiran alcoholic beverage
Parades, often with music and dancing, part of the festivals

dialogues.title: Festival Plans
dialogues.context: Maria and João, neighbours, are chatting outside their homes about the upcoming June festivals.

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Olá, João! Tudo bem? Já a pensar nos Santos Populares?
lines.translation: Hello, João! All good? Already thinking about the Popular Saints' festivals?

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Olá, Maria! Pois é, claro que sim! É a melhor altura do ano, não é? Tu vais a alguma festa?
lines.translation: Hello, Maria! That's right, of course! It's the best time of the year, isn't it? Are you going to any festival?

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Vou sim! Gostava de ir à festa de São João na Ponta do Sol. Dizem que as marchas lá são incríveis.
lines.translation: Yes, I am! I'd like to go to the São João festival in Ponta do Sol. They say the parades there are incredible.

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Ah, sim, é verdade! E as sardinhas assadas, já provaste as de lá? São divinais!
lines.translation: Ah, yes, that's true! And the grilled sardines, have you tried the ones from there? They're divine!

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Ainda não, mas está nos planos! E tu, vais ao arraial de Santo António aqui perto?
lines.translation: Not yet, but it's in the plans! And you, are you going to the Santo António outdoor festival nearby?

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Claro que sim! É uma tradição muito bonita. Não perco por nada. Se fores, diz-me!
lines.translation: Of course! It's a very beautiful tradition. I wouldn't miss it for anything. If you go, tell me!

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Combinado! Quem sabe não nos encontramos por lá?
lines.translation: Agreed! Who knows, maybe we'll meet there?

cultural_notes.title: The Santos Populares in Madeira
cultural_notes.body: June is a month of celebration in Madeira, known for the 'Santos Populares' (Popular Saints' festivals). These include Santo António (June 13th), São João (June 24th), and São Pedro (June 29th). Each village and parish has its own 'festa' or 'arraial' (outdoor festival) with unique traditions. Common elements include lively music, folk dancing, 'marchas' (parades), bonfires (especially for São João), and plenty of traditional food like 'sardinhas assadas' (grilled sardines), 'espetada' (beef skewers), and 'bolo do caco' (Madeiran flatbread). It's a time for community, family, and friends to gather and celebrate.

cultural_notes.title: Joining Local Festivities
cultural_notes.body: As a newcomer, showing interest in the 'Santos Populares' is a great way to connect with locals and feel part of the community. People are generally very proud of their traditions and happy to share them. Don't be shy to ask about specific festivals, what food to try, or where the best 'arraiais' are. Expressing your enjoyment of the festivities will be well-received and can open doors to deeper conversations and friendships.

roleplay.scenario: You're at your local café in Madeira, enjoying a 'bica'. A friendly neighbour, Dona Fátima, strikes up a conversation about the upcoming Santos Populares. She's keen to know if you're joining in the festivities.

nodes.npc_text: Olá! Já a pensar nos Santos Populares? É uma das melhores alturas do ano, não é? Há sempre tanta coisa para fazer!
nodes.npc_translation: Hello! Already thinking about the Popular Saints' festivals? It's one of the best times of the year, isn't it? There's always so much to do!

options.text: Olá, Dona Fátima! Sim, é verdade. Estou muito entusiasmado/a.
options.translation: Hello, Dona Fátima! Yes, it's true. I'm very excited.
options.feedback: Great start! You acknowledged her and expressed enthusiasm.

options.text: Olá! Sim, parece uma tradição muito bonita. Quais são os seus planos?
options.translation: Hello! Yes, it seems like a very beautiful tradition. What are your plans?
options.feedback: Good! You showed interest and asked about her plans.

nodes.npc_text: Que bom! Já pensou em ir a alguma festa em particular? A de São João na Ponta do Sol é sempre muito animada, com as marchas e tudo.
nodes.npc_translation: That's good! Have you thought about going to any particular festival? The São João one in Ponta do Sol is always very lively, with the parades and everything.

options.text: Sim, gostava de ir à festa de São João. Ouvi dizer que é muito boa.
options.translation: Yes, I'd like to go to the São João festival. I heard it's very good.
options.feedback: Excellent! You confirmed your interest and referred to what you've heard.

options.text: Ainda não sei bem. Há alguma festa que me recomende?
options.translation: I don't know yet. Is there any festival you recommend?
options.feedback: Good! Asking for a recommendation is a natural way to continue the conversation.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, os meus planos... Bem, eu vou sempre ao arraial de Santo António aqui perto. É onde a gente se encontra com os vizinhos e come umas sardinhas. Já comeu sardinhas assadas?
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, my plans... Well, I always go to the Santo António outdoor festival nearby. It's where we meet up with neighbours and eat some sardines. Have you already eaten grilled sardines?

options.text: Sim, adoro sardinhas assadas! É uma das minhas coisas favoritas.
options.translation: Yes, I love grilled sardines! It's one of my favourite things.
options.feedback: Perfect! You shared your opinion and connected with her experience.

options.text: Ainda não, mas gostava de provar. Onde posso encontrar as melhores?
options.translation: Not yet, but I'd like to try them. Where can I find the best ones?
options.feedback: Good! You expressed curiosity and asked for advice.

nodes.npc_text: Pois é! É uma delícia. Se for à festa de São João ou ao arraial de Santo António, diga-me. Quem sabe não nos encontramos por lá? Seria giro!
nodes.npc_translation: That's right! It's a delight. If you go to the São João festival or the Santo António outdoor festival, tell me. Who knows, maybe we'll meet there? That would be nice!

options.text: Combinado! Obrigada pela sugestão, Dona Fátima.
options.translation: Agreed! Thank you for the suggestion, Dona Fátima.
options.feedback: Excellent! You accepted the invitation and thanked her politely. Conversation ends naturally.

nodes.npc_text: Bem, para sardinhas assadas, o arraial de Santo António aqui perto é sempre uma boa aposta. Mas a festa de São Pedro no Porto Moniz também é famosa pela espetada. É uma tradição muito importante para nós. Pense nisso!
nodes.npc_translation: Well, for grilled sardines, the Santo António outdoor festival nearby is always a good bet. But the São Pedro festival in Porto Moniz is also famous for its espetada. It's a very important tradition for us. Think about it!

options.text: Muito obrigada pelas dicas! Vou pensar nisso e talvez a gente se encontre.
options.translation: Thank you very much for the tips! I'll think about it and maybe we'll meet up.
options.feedback: Perfect! You thanked her for the advice and kept the possibility of meeting open. Conversation ends naturally.

sit-d96-vindima-harvest.title: Vindima & the Harvest
sit-d96-vindima-harvest.summary: Harvest time: grapes for Vinho Madeira, helping a neighbour with the vindima, harvest lunches, and the vocabulary of vines, baskets and pressing.

phrase_patterns.base: Já está a preparar-se p'ra vindima?
phrase_patterns.translation: Are you already preparing for the grape harvest?

variants.text: Já se está a preparar para a vindima?
variants.translation: Are you already preparing for the grape harvest?
variants.note: Slightly more formal, less common reduction 'p'ra'

phrase_patterns.base: Podia dar uma ajuda com a vindima?
phrase_patterns.translation: Could you help with the grape harvest?

variants.translation: I'd like to ask for some help for the grape harvest.

vocabulary.note: The act of harvesting grapes
Place where grapes are pressed to make wine or must
Traditional Madeiran bread, often served with garlic butter or cane honey
Traditional Madeiran dish, usually beef on a laurel skewer
Madeiran regionalism for 'batata'

dialogues.title: Harvest Talk
dialogues.context: Two neighbours, Dona Laura and Sr. Manuel, are chatting by the roadside about the upcoming grape harvest.

lines.speaker: Dona Laura
lines.text: Bom dia, Sr. Manuel! Tudo bem? Já está a preparar-se p'ra vindima?
lines.translation: Good morning, Mr. Manuel! All good? Are you already preparing for the grape harvest?

lines.speaker: Sr. Manuel
lines.text: Pois é, Dona Laura! Este ano a colheita promete, mas precisamos de mais mãos. A sua vizinha também vai ajudar.
lines.translation: That's right, Dona Laura! This year the harvest looks promising, but we need more hands. Your neighbour is also going to help.

lines.speaker: Dona Laura
lines.text: Ah, que bom! Eu posso dar uma ajuda, sem problema. E depois um almoço com espetada, não?
lines.translation: Oh, that's good! I can help, no problem. And afterwards a lunch with espetada, right?

lines.speaker: Sr. Manuel
lines.text: Claro! E bolo do caco com manteiga de alho. E um copo de vinho novo no lagar! Que tal no sábado de manhã?
lines.translation: Of course! And bolo do caco with garlic butter. And a glass of new wine in the pressing room! How about Saturday morning?

lines.speaker: Dona Laura
lines.text: Perfeito! Contem comigo. Até sábado, então!
lines.translation: Perfect! Count me in. See you Saturday, then!

cultural_notes.title: The Communal Vindima
cultural_notes.body: In Madeira, the grape harvest (vindima) is often a community event, especially in rural areas. Families and neighbours frequently help each other, sharing the work and the joy. It's common for these efforts to be rewarded with a generous shared meal afterwards, celebrating the hard work and the bounty of the land. This tradition reinforces community bonds and local identity.

cultural_notes.title: Harvest Meals and Treats
cultural_notes.body: Harvest lunches are a significant part of the vindima experience. Traditional Madeiran dishes like espetada (beef skewers), bolo do caco (flatbread often with garlic butter), and semilhas (potatoes) are commonly served. There's also the anticipation of 'vinho novo' (new wine) or 'mosto' (must) directly from the lagar (wine press), a fresh taste of the year's yield.

roleplay.scenario: Your neighbour, Sr. João, approaches you while you're tending to your garden. He's looking for help with his family's grape harvest this weekend.

nodes.npc_text: Olá! Tudo bem? Olhe, ia perguntar se me podia dar uma ajuda com a vindima este fim de semana? Precisamos de mais mãos lá no lagar.
nodes.npc_translation: Hello! All good? Look, I was going to ask if you could help me with the grape harvest this weekend? We need more hands down at the pressing room.

options.text: Claro, Sr. João! Em que dia e a que horas?
options.translation: Of course, Mr. João! On what day and at what time?
options.feedback: Great start! You've agreed to help and asked for important details.

options.text: Este fim de semana é um bocado complicado, mas posso noutro dia.
options.translation: This weekend is a bit complicated, but I can on another day.
options.feedback: Good, you've politely declined for now but offered an alternative.

nodes.npc_text: Pois é, no sábado de manhã, lá p'ra as nove. Depois há almoço no lagar, com bolo do caco e espetada. É p'ra não faltar!
nodes.npc_translation: That's right, Saturday morning, around nine. Afterwards there's lunch at the pressing room, with bolo do caco and espetada. You can't miss it!

options.text: Combinado! Levo alguma coisa para ajudar?
options.translation: Agreed! Should I bring anything to help?
options.feedback: Excellent! You've confirmed and offered to contribute, showing initiative.

options.text: Perfeito! Até sábado, então. Muito obrigado pelo convite.
options.translation: Perfect! See you Saturday, then. Thank you very much for the invitation.
options.feedback: Solid confirmation and polite thanks. You're all set!

nodes.npc_text: Ah, que pena! E na próxima semana, consegue? É que precisamos mesmo de mais mãos, a colheita está boa.
nodes.npc_translation: Oh, what a pity! And next week, can you manage? We really need more hands, the harvest is good.

options.text: Sim, na próxima semana é melhor. Que dia sugere?
options.translation: Yes, next week is better. What day do you suggest?
options.feedback: Good, you've found a solution and asked for details.

options.text: Infelizmente, estou mesmo ocupado. Mas obrigado pelo convite!
options.translation: Unfortunately, I'm really busy. But thank you for the invitation!
options.feedback: A polite but firm refusal. It's okay to say no sometimes!

nodes.npc_text: Pode ser na terça, de tarde? Umas três horas. A minha mulher vai fazer umas sandes de atum e sumo.
nodes.npc_translation: Could it be on Tuesday, in the afternoon? Around three o'clock. My wife is going to make some tuna sandwiches and juice.

options.text: Sim, terça à tarde funciona. Contem comigo!
options.translation: Yes, Tuesday afternoon works. Count me in!
options.feedback: Excellent! You've confirmed the new plan.

options.text: Perfeito! Até terça, então. Obrigado!
options.translation: Perfect! See you Tuesday, then. Thanks!
options.feedback: Good, you've confirmed and thanked him.

nodes.npc_text: Muito obrigado pela ajuda! Tenha um bom dia!
nodes.npc_translation: Thank you very much for the help! Have a good day!

nodes.npc_text: Ah, que pena! Fica p'ra próxima. Bom dia!
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, what a pity! Maybe next time. Good day!

sit-d97-inviting-to-a-festa.title: Inviting & Being Invited
sit-d97-inviting-to-a-festa.summary: Festa invitations done right: inviting neighbours, accepting warmly, asking what to bring, arriving at the right time (which is not the stated time), and thanking afterwards.

phrase_patterns.translation: Do you want to come to my party {day}?

variants.translation: Do you want to come over {day}?
variants.note: More general, less formal than 'festa'

variants.translation: Do you want me to bring something?
Is there anything I can bring?

phrase_patterns.translation: Thank you very much for the invitation!

vocabulary.note: Madeira regionalism
Madeira regionalism
Madeira regionalism

dialogues.title: Festa Invitation
dialogues.context: Maria invites her neighbour, João, to a small festa at her home in Madeira.

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Olá, João! Tudo bem? Olha, estamos a organizar uma festinha cá em casa no sábado. Queres vir?
lines.translation: Hi, João! How are you? Look, we're organizing a small party here at home on Saturday. Do you want to come?

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Ah, Maria, que bom! Claro que sim! A que horas é?
lines.translation: Oh, Maria, how nice! Of course! What time is it?

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Olha, começa p'raí às sete, mas chega por volta das oito, nove… Sabes como é!
lines.translation: Look, it starts around seven, but arrive around eight, nine... You know how it is!

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Pois, pois! E queres que leve alguma coisa? Um bolo do caco, talvez?
lines.translation: Right, right! And do you want me to bring anything? A bolo do caco, maybe?

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Não te preocupes! Mas se quiseres, uma garrafa de vinho é sempre bem-vinda. Ou um sumo p'ra criançada.
lines.translation: Don't worry! But if you want, a bottle of wine is always welcome. Or some juice for the kids.

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Combinado! Muito obrigado pelo convite, Maria! Até sábado!
lines.translation: Deal! Thank you very much for the invitation, Maria! See you Saturday!

cultural_notes.title: Portuguese Time (Hora Portuguesa)
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, especially in informal social gatherings, it's common for guests to arrive later than the stated time. If a party is advertised for 7 PM, arriving at 8 PM or even 9 PM is often perfectly acceptable, sometimes even expected. This is known as 'hora portuguesa' (Portuguese time). It allows the hosts extra time for last-minute preparations without feeling rushed and avoids guests arriving before everything is ready.

cultural_notes.title: Bringing a Small Gift to a Festa
cultural_notes.body: While not strictly obligatory, it's a kind gesture to bring a small gift when invited to someone's home for a party. This could be a bottle of wine, a dessert, some local Madeiran speciality like a bolo do caco, or a small box of chocolates. The host will usually say 'Não te preocupes!' (Don't worry!) but a small contribution is almost always appreciated and shows good manners.

roleplay.scenario: Your neighbour, Dona Laura, has just invited you to a small gathering at her house this Sunday. You want to accept politely and offer to bring something.

nodes.npc_text: Olá! Olha, no domingo vamos ter uma pequena festa lá em casa. Queres aparecer?
nodes.npc_translation: Hi! Look, on Sunday we're having a small party at our place. Do you want to come?

options.text: Sim, claro! Que ótimo! A que horas?
options.translation: Yes, of course! How great! What time?
options.feedback: Good, you accepted and asked for the time. This is a natural next step.

options.text: Ah, que pena, não posso. Tenho um compromisso.
options.translation: Oh, what a shame, I can't. I have an engagement.
options.feedback: A polite refusal. It's good to have a reason, even vague.

nodes.npc_text: Olha, a festa começa p'raí às sete, mas não te preocupes em chegar logo. Aparece por volta das oito, oito e meia.
nodes.npc_translation: Look, the party starts around seven, but don't worry about arriving right away. Come around eight, half past eight.

options.text: Combinado! E queres que leve alguma coisa? Um sumo ou um bolo?
options.translation: Understood! And do you want me to bring anything? A juice or a cake?
options.feedback: Excellent! You confirmed the time and offered to bring something, with specific suggestions.

options.text: Está bem! Muito obrigado pelo convite!
options.translation: Okay! Thank you very much for the invitation!
options.feedback: A good acceptance, but you missed the chance to offer to bring something, which is a common courtesy.

nodes.npc_text: Não, não te preocupes! Mas se insistes, um bom vinho madeirense é sempre bem-vindo. Ou uns rebuçados de mel.
nodes.npc_translation: No, don't worry! But if you insist, a good Madeiran wine is always welcome. Or some honey candies.

options.text: Perfeito! Então levo um vinho. Até domingo, Dona Laura!
options.translation: Perfect! I'll bring a wine then. See you Sunday, Dona Laura!
options.feedback: Great! You accepted the suggestion and confirmed. Very natural ending.

options.text: Está bem, não levo nada então. Muito obrigado!
options.translation: Okay, I won't bring anything then. Thank you very much!
options.feedback: You could have insisted a little more, but this is still polite. The host's initial 'não te preocupes' is often a polite refusal.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, que pena! Fica para a próxima então. Um abraço!
nodes.npc_translation: Oh, what a shame! Next time then. A hug!

nodes.npc_text: De nada! Até domingo!
nodes.npc_translation: You're welcome! See you Sunday!

nodes.npc_text: Até domingo! Obrigada!
nodes.npc_translation: See you Sunday! Thank you!

sit-d98-week-14-stress-test.title: Week 14 Stress Test
sit-d98-week-14-stress-test.summary: A festival weekend end-to-end: getting invited, planning the day, navigating the arraial crowd, festival small talk and the goodbyes — month-4 culture under pressure.

phrase_patterns.base: Vais ao arraial {location} este fim de semana?
phrase_patterns.translation: Are you going to the festival at {location} this weekend?

variants.text: Não queres ir ao arraial {location} este fim de semana?
variants.translation: Don't you want to go to the festival at {location} this weekend?

variants.text: Isto está cheio de gente, não está?
variants.translation: This is full of people, isn't it?

slots.name: food_item
options: bolo do caco com manteiga de alho

vocabulary.note: Common term in Portugal, especially Madeira, for local religious festivals.
Used to describe a very large crowd of people.
Madeiran alcoholic drink made with aguardente (sugarcane rum), honey, and lemon juice.
Traditional Madeiran dish of beef cubes on a laurel skewer, grilled.
Traditional Madeiran wheat flour bread, often served with garlic butter.
Common in pt-PT for 'very much' or 'a lot'.

dialogues.title: Invitation to the Arraial
dialogues.context: You meet your neighbour, Dona Maria, near the local market. She's asking about your weekend plans.

lines.speaker: Dona Maria
lines.text: Olá! Tudo bem? Vais ao arraial este fim de semana, não vais?
lines.translation: Hello! All good? You're going to the festival this weekend, aren't you?

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: Olá, Dona Maria! Sim, estou a pensar ir. Qual é que é o melhor dia?
lines.translation: Hello, Dona Maria! Yes, I'm thinking of going. Which is the best day?

lines.speaker: Dona Maria
lines.text: Ah, o sábado à noite é sempre o mais animado. Há mais música e mais gente.
lines.translation: Ah, Saturday night is always the liveliest. There's more music and more people.

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: Pois, imagino. E a comida? O que é que me aconselha a provar?
lines.translation: Right, I imagine. And the food? What do you advise me to try?

lines.speaker: Dona Maria
lines.text: Tens que comer uma espetada e um bolo do caco, claro! E uma poncha para refrescar.
lines.translation: You have to eat an espetada and a bolo do caco, of course! And a poncha to refresh.

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: Hmm, parece-me bem! Obrigado pelas dicas, Dona Maria.
lines.translation: Hmm, sounds good! Thanks for the tips, Dona Maria.

lines.speaker: Dona Maria
lines.text: De nada! Se me vires lá, diz olá! Fica bem!
lines.translation: You're welcome! If you see me there, say hello! Take care!

dialogues.title: Festival Small Talk
dialogues.context: You are at a crowded arraial, trying to get a poncha. You bump into your friend, Pedro.

lines.speaker: Pedro
lines.text: Ora, quem é que eu encontro aqui! Tudo bem? Isto está uma enchente, não está?
lines.translation: Well, who do I find here! All good? This is packed, isn't it?

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: Pedro! Olá! Pois é, está uma confusão tremenda! Estou há imenso tempo à espera de uma poncha.
lines.translation: Pedro! Hello! That's right, it's incredibly crowded! I've been waiting for a poncha for ages.

lines.speaker: Pedro
lines.text: Eu sei, é sempre assim. Mas vale a pena. Já provaste a espetada?
lines.translation: I know, it's always like that. But it's worth it. Have you tried the espetada yet?

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: Ainda não, mas está nos planos. A música está boa, não está?
lines.translation: Not yet, but it's in the plans. The music is good, isn't it?

lines.speaker: Pedro
lines.text: Está sim! É pena que seja tão difícil andar por aqui. Queres ir apanhar um ar lá fora um bocado?
lines.translation: It is! It's a shame it's so difficult to walk around here. Do you want to go get some fresh air outside for a bit?

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: Sim, boa ideia! Acabando a poncha, vamos.
lines.translation: Yes, good idea! Once I finish the poncha, let's go.

lines.speaker: Pedro
lines.text: Combinado. Até já, então.
lines.translation: Deal. See you in a bit, then.

cultural_notes.title: The Madeiran Arraial
cultural_notes.body: An 'arraial' in Madeira is a traditional local festival, often associated with a religious celebration (like a saint's day). They are vibrant community events featuring live music (often traditional 'bandas filarmónicas' or folk groups), food stalls selling Madeiran specialties like espetada, bolo do caco, and carne de vinho e alhos, and of course, poncha. They are incredibly popular and can get very crowded, especially on Saturday nights. It's a key part of Madeiran social life and a great place to experience local culture, meet friends, and enjoy the festive atmosphere.

cultural_notes.title: Navigating Crowds and Small Talk
cultural_notes.body: At an arraial, expect large crowds ('enchentes' or 'confusões'). It's common to comment on the number of people. Small talk often revolves around the food, drinks, music, and the general atmosphere. Don't be afraid to strike up a conversation with locals; they are usually very friendly. 'Pois é' is a common and natural way to agree or acknowledge what someone has said in European Portuguese.

roleplay.scenario: You are at a bustling arraial in Madeira. You've just finished a bolo do caco and are looking for a drink. Your friend, Sofia, spots you.

nodes.npc_text: Olá! Que surpresa boa! Estás a gostar do arraial?
nodes.npc_translation: Hello! What a nice surprise! Are you enjoying the festival?

options.text: Sim, estou a gostar imenso! Está muita gente, não está?
options.translation: Yes, I'm enjoying it immensely! It's very crowded, isn't it?
options.feedback: Great start, acknowledging the crowd is very natural.

options.text: Está bom, mas está um bocado cheio. Onde é que posso arranjar uma poncha?
options.translation: It's good, but it's a bit full. Where can I get a poncha?
options.feedback: Good question! Direct and practical for the situation.

nodes.npc_text: Pois é, está uma enchente! É normal, é dos arraiais mais populares. Já provaste a espetada daqui?
nodes.npc_translation: That's right, it's packed! It's normal, it's one of the most popular festivals. Have you tried the espetada from here yet?

options.text: Ainda não, mas parece deliciosa. E tu, o que é que já comeste?
options.translation: Not yet, but it looks delicious. And you, what have you eaten already?
options.feedback: Excellent, turning the question back to Sofia keeps the conversation flowing.

options.text: Não, já comi um bolo do caco. Agora queria uma bebida para refrescar.
options.translation: No, I already ate a bolo do caco. Now I want a drink to refresh.
options.feedback: Good, you're stating your current need. Sofia will help.

nodes.npc_text: Claro, há uma banca ali mais à frente que faz ponchas muito boas. Queres que te acompanhe?
nodes.npc_translation: Of course, there's a stall a bit further ahead that makes very good ponchas. Do you want me to go with you?

options.text: Sim, por favor! Assim não me perco nesta multidão.
options.translation: Yes, please! That way I won't get lost in this crowd.
options.feedback: Polite and practical, accepting the help is a good social move.

options.text: Não é preciso, obrigado/a. Mas obrigado/a pela dica!
options.translation: No need, thanks. But thanks for the tip!
options.feedback: Polite refusal, but you might miss out on more interaction.

nodes.npc_text: Eu já comi uma espetada e umas lapas. Tudo uma delícia! Mas está mesmo difícil falar com este barulho.
nodes.npc_translation: I've already eaten an espetada and some limpets. All delicious! But it's really hard to talk with this noise.

options.text: Pois é, o barulho é imenso. Talvez seja melhor ir para um sítio mais calmo.
options.translation: That's right, the noise is immense. Maybe it's better to go to a quieter place.
options.feedback: Good observation and suggestion, showing you're engaged.

options.text: Sim, e a fila para as bebidas é enorme. Acho que vou para casa.
options.translation: Yes, and the queue for drinks is huge. I think I'm going home.
options.feedback: A bit abrupt, but understandable if you're tired of the crowd.

nodes.npc_text: Boa ideia! Vamos lá para a zona dos bancos, que é mais tranquilo. Assim podemos conversar melhor. Anda!
nodes.npc_translation: Good idea! Let's go to the seating area, it's calmer there. That way we can talk better. Come on!

nodes.npc_text: Ah, que pena! Mas percebo. Então, até à próxima! Fica bem!
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, what a shame! But I understand. Well, see you next time! Take care!

sit-d99-espetada-night.summary: Dinner at a casa de pasto: ordering espetada em pau de louro, milho frito and salada, sharing dishes, asking for more bolo do caco, and the long-table conversation around it.

variants.note: Slightly less direct, but still polite.
More informal, common with 'tu' address.

Used as the skewer for traditional Madeiran espetada.

dialogues.title: Dinner at the Casa de Pasto
dialogues.context: At a busy traditional Madeiran restaurant, a group of friends and neighbours are having dinner. The waiter is taking their order.

lines.speaker: Empregado
lines.text: Boa noite. Já decidiram o que vão comer?
lines.translation: Good evening. Have you decided what you're going to eat?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Boa noite! Sim, eu queria uma espetada em pau de louro, por favor. E uma dose de milho frito para a mesa.
lines.translation: Good evening! Yes, I'd like a laurel-stick espetada, please. And a portion of fried cornmeal for the table.

lines.speaker: Vizinha
lines.text: Ah, e eu queria só uma salada mista. E mais bolo do caco para todos, sim?
lines.translation: Oh, and I'd just like a mixed salad. And more bolo do caco for everyone, yes?

lines.speaker: Empregado
lines.text: P'ra já! E para beber?
lines.translation: Coming right up! And to drink?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Para mim, um copo de vinho da casa. E a espetada está a ficar mesmo tenra, esta noite!
lines.translation: For me, a glass of house wine. And the espetada is getting really tender tonight!

lines.speaker: Vizinha
lines.text: Pois é, está uma delícia! Vamos dividir a salada, depois?
lines.translation: Indeed, it's delicious! Shall we share the salad, later?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Claro! E o milho frito também, se sobrar. Está muito saboroso.
lines.translation: Of course! And the fried cornmeal too, if there's any left. It's very tasty.

cultural_notes.title: Espetada and Sharing in Madeira
cultural_notes.body: Espetada em pau de louro is a quintessential Madeiran dish. Traditionally, beef cubes are skewered onto a laurel (louro) stick, which imparts a unique flavour, and grilled over charcoal. It's common for side dishes like milho frito (fried cornmeal cubes) and bolo do caco (Madeiran flatbread with garlic butter) to be ordered 'para a mesa' (for the table) and shared amongst diners. This communal eating style is a big part of the dining experience in a 'casa de pasto' (traditional eatery).

cultural_notes.title: Polite Requests and Reductions
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, 'queria' (I would like) and 'podia trazer' (could you bring) are polite and common ways to order or make requests, even in informal settings like a local restaurant. You'll also often hear reductions like 'p'ra já' (for now/coming right up) from service staff, which is a natural part of spoken Portuguese in Madeira.

roleplay.scenario: You are at a local Madeiran 'casa de pasto' with a friend. The waiter approaches to take your order.

nodes.npc_text: Boa noite! O que vão querer hoje?
nodes.npc_translation: Good evening! What will you have today?

options.text: Queria uma espetada em pau de louro, por favor.
options.translation: I'd like a laurel-stick espetada, please.
options.feedback: Excellent, polite and specific! Remember 'em pau de louro' for the authentic Madeiran touch.

options.text: Vou querer só uma salada mista.
options.translation: I'll just have a mixed salad.
options.feedback: Good, clear order. Now try to add a side dish for the table!

nodes.npc_text: Ótima escolha! E alguma coisa para acompanhar, ou para a mesa?
nodes.npc_translation: Great choice! And anything to go with it, or for the table?

options.text: Sim, uma dose de milho frito para a mesa, por favor.
options.translation: Yes, a portion of fried cornmeal for the table, please.
options.feedback: Perfeito! 'Para a mesa' is exactly how you'd order shared dishes.

options.text: Não, está bom assim, obrigado.
options.translation: No, that's fine, thank you.
options.feedback: That's fine, but in Madeira, sharing is caring! Try ordering a side next time.

nodes.npc_text: Uma salada mista. Mais alguma coisa?
nodes.npc_translation: A mixed salad. Anything else?

options.text: Podia trazer também bolo do caco para a mesa?
options.translation: Could you also bring bolo do caco for the table?
options.feedback: Very good! 'Podia trazer' is a polite way to ask, and 'bolo do caco' is a must!

nodes.npc_text: P'ra já! Em breve a vossa comida está a chegar.
nodes.npc_translation: Coming right up! Your food will be arriving soon.

options.text: Muito obrigado!
options.translation: Thank you very much!
options.feedback: Perfect, a polite and natural response. Enjoy your meal!

dialogues.title: Ordering Lunch
dialogues.context: A customer is ordering lunch at a traditional Madeiran restaurant.

lines.speaker: Empregado
lines.text: Boa tarde! Já decidiu o que vai comer?
lines.translation: Good afternoon! Have you decided what you're going to eat?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Boa tarde! Estou um bocado indeciso. O que é que me recomenda hoje?
lines.translation: Good afternoon! I'm a bit undecided. What do you recommend today?

lines.speaker: Empregado
lines.text: Bem, temos um bife de atum fresquinho, que está uma maravilha. Ou se preferir algo mais tradicional, o picado de carne é sempre uma boa opção.
lines.translation: Well, we have a very fresh tuna steak, which is wonderful. Or if you prefer something more traditional, the beef picado is always a good option.

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: O picado é para partilhar, certo?
lines.translation: The picado is for sharing, right?

lines.speaker: Empregado
lines.text: Sim, o picado normalmente é para duas pessoas. Traz carne de vaca aos cubos, com batata frita e um molho de alho.
lines.translation: Yes, the picado is usually for two people. It comes with diced beef, with fries and a garlic sauce.

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Hmm, parece bem. Mas hoje queria algo só para mim. Acho que vou querer o bife de atum. Como é que ele vem?
lines.translation: Hmm, sounds good. But today I wanted something just for myself. I think I'll have the tuna steak. How does it come?

lines.speaker: Empregado
lines.text: O bife de atum vem grelhado, com milho frito e salada. Pode escolher se quer bem passado, médio ou mal passado.
lines.translation: The tuna steak comes grilled, with fried cornmeal and salad. You can choose if you want it well-done, medium, or rare.

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Perfeito! Médio, por favor. E para beber, uma água sem gás.
lines.translation: Perfect! Medium, please. And to drink, a still water.

lines.speaker: Empregado
lines.text: Pois, muito bem. Já vem!
lines.translation: Right, very good. Coming right up!

cultural_notes.title: Madeiran Sharing Culture
cultural_notes.body: In Madeira, many dishes are traditionally served for sharing, especially among friends or family. The 'picado' is a prime example, often served on a large platter in the middle of the table, allowing everyone to pick from it. 'Espetada' can also be shared. It's common to ask 'É para partilhar?' or 'Dá para partilhar?' to understand portion sizes and dining customs.

cultural_notes.title: Polite Ordering with 'Queria'
cultural_notes.body: When ordering food or drinks in Portugal, it's very common and polite to use the conditional 'Queria...' (I would like...) instead of the direct 'Eu quero...' (I want...). This softens the request and is considered more courteous in service interactions. For example, 'Queria um café, por favor' is preferred over 'Eu quero um café, por favor'.

roleplay.scenario: You're at a popular local 'tasca' (tavern) in Funchal, looking at the menu. The waiter approaches to take your order.

nodes.npc_text: Boa noite! Já sabe o que vai querer?
nodes.npc_translation: Good evening! Do you already know what you'd like?

options.text: Boa noite! Ainda não. O que é que me recomenda?
options.translation: Good evening! Not yet. What do you recommend?
options.feedback: Excellent! Asking for recommendations is a great way to engage and discover local favorites.

options.text: Boa noite! Sim, queria um prego no bolo do caco.
options.translation: Good evening! Yes, I'd like a prego no bolo do caco.
options.feedback: Good direct order, polite use of 'queria'. Now, what about a drink?

nodes.npc_text: Hoje temos umas lapas grelhadas muito boas, e o nosso bife de atum com milho frito é sempre um sucesso. Ou se preferir carne, a espetada é excelente.
nodes.npc_translation: Today we have some very good grilled limpets, and our tuna steak with fried cornmeal is always a success. Or if you prefer meat, the espetada is excellent.

options.text: As lapas grelhadas vêm com o quê?
options.translation: What do the grilled limpets come with?
options.feedback: Great question! Asking about accompaniments helps you understand the dish better.

options.text: Obrigado! Acho que vou querer o bife de atum. Médio, por favor.
options.translation: Thanks! I think I'll have the tuna steak. Medium, please.
options.feedback: Perfect! You've made a choice and specified your preference. Now for drinks!

nodes.npc_text: Muito bem. E para beber, o que é que vai ser?
nodes.npc_translation: Very good. And to drink, what will it be?

options.text: Queria uma imperial, por favor.
options.translation: I'd like a draft beer, please.
options.feedback: Excellent! You've successfully ordered your meal and a drink. Enjoy!

options.text: Uma água sem gás, por favor.
options.translation: A still water, please.
options.feedback: Great! A simple and clear order. Well done!

nodes.npc_text: As lapas vêm grelhadas na chapa, com manteiga de alho e limão. São ótimas para entrada!
nodes.npc_translation: The limpets come grilled on the griddle, with garlic butter and lemon. They're great as a starter!

options.text: Parece delicioso! Queria as lapas e depois o bife de atum, médio.
options.translation: Sounds delicious! I'd like the limpets and then the tuna steak, medium.
options.feedback: Fantastic! You've ordered an appetizer and a main course, clearly stating your preferences.

options.text: Ah, ok. Acho que vou direto para o bife de atum. Médio, por favor.
options.translation: Ah, okay. I think I'll go straight for the tuna steak. Medium, please.
options.feedback: Good choice! You've decided on your main dish. What about a drink?

nodes.npc_text: Pois, muito bem. Já vem!
nodes.npc_translation: Right, very good. Coming right up!

sit-d101-talking-recipes.title: Talking Recipes & How It’s Made
sit-d101-talking-recipes.summary: Food talk beyond the menu: how bolo do caco is made, what goes in a poncha, a neighbour’s recipe — following instructions (amassa-se, deixa-se descansar) and asking about steps.

variants.translation: You let it rest for a bit.

slots.name: question_phrase
slots.description: A phrase to ask what happens next

vocabulary.note: Madeira regionalism for 'batata'

dialogues.title: Bolo do Caco Recipe
dialogues.context: Two neighbours, Maria and João, are chatting outside their homes. João is asking Maria about her bolo do caco recipe.

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Olá, Maria! O teu bolo do caco é sempre o melhor. Qual é o segredo?
lines.translation: Hi, Maria! Your bolo do caco is always the best. What's the secret?

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Olá, João! Ah, não tem segredo nenhum. É a receita da minha avó. Leva farinha, água, levedura e um bocadinho de sal.
lines.translation: Hi, João! Ah, there's no secret. It's my grandmother's recipe. It takes flour, water, yeast, and a little bit of salt.

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Pois, e depois? Amassa-se por muito tempo?
lines.translation: Right, and then? Do you knead it for a long time?

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Sim, amassa-se bem, até ficar fofa. Depois, deixa-se descansar a massa num sítio quentinho, p'ra levedar.
lines.translation: Yes, you knead it well, until it's soft. Then, you let the dough rest in a warm place, to rise.

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: E depois de levedar, coze-se no forno?
lines.translation: And after it rises, do you bake it in the oven?

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Não, não! O bolo do caco coze-se numa chapa quente, não no forno. Fica mais estaladiço!
lines.translation: No, no! Bolo do caco is cooked on a hot griddle, not in the oven. It gets crispier!

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Ah, pois é! Que diferença. Tenho de experimentar isso, obrigado!
lines.translation: Ah, that's right! What a difference. I have to try that, thanks!

cultural_notes.title: Sharing Recipes and the Impersonal 'Se'
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, especially in informal settings like among neighbours, sharing recipes is common. When explaining cooking steps, the impersonal 'se' (e.g., 'faz-se', 'amassa-se', 'deixa-se') is frequently used. It translates to 'one does', 'you do', or 'it is done', and is a very natural way to give instructions without specifying who performs the action. It's crucial for understanding and giving cooking directions.

cultural_notes.title: Madeiran Food & Social Bonds
cultural_notes.body: Food is central to Madeiran culture and social life. Conversations about how dishes are made, family recipes, and local ingredients like 'semilha' (potato) are common and help strengthen community bonds. Asking for a recipe from a neighbour is a sign of connection and appreciation.

roleplay.scenario: You're at the local market in Funchal, buying vegetables. You see a neighbour, Dona Rosa, known for her delicious 'espetada' (Madeiran beef skewer). You decide to ask her about her recipe.

nodes.npc_text: Olá! Tudo bem? Estás a comprar umas coisas boas, não é?
nodes.npc_translation: Hello! All good? You're buying some good things, aren't you?

options.text: Olá, Dona Rosa! Tudo bem, sim. Estou a tentar fazer uma espetada este fim de semana.
options.translation: Hello, Dona Rosa! All good, yes. I'm trying to make 'espetada' this weekend.
options.feedback: Good start! You've set the context for your question.

options.text: Sim, Dona Rosa. Mas estou a pensar na sua espetada... É a melhor!
options.translation: Yes, Dona Rosa. But I'm thinking about your 'espetada'... It's the best!
options.feedback: Flattery works! You've opened the door to asking about the recipe.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, a minha espetada! Obrigada! É simples, mas tem os seus truques. O que queres saber?
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, my 'espetada'! Thank you! It's simple, but it has its tricks. What do you want to know?

options.text: Que carne se usa? E leva alho?
options.translation: What meat do you use? And does it take garlic?
options.feedback: Excellent, asking about key ingredients.

options.text: Como é que se faz para a carne ficar tão tenra?
options.translation: How do you make the meat so tender?
options.feedback: Good question about a specific quality of the dish.

nodes.npc_text: Usa-se carne de vaca, cortada em cubos grandes. E sim, leva imenso alho esmagado, louro e sal grosso. Deixa-se marinar umas horas.
nodes.npc_translation: You use beef, cut into large cubes. And yes, it takes lots of crushed garlic, bay leaf, and coarse salt. You let it marinate for a few hours.

options.text: Ah, entendi! E depois, como é que se faz para cozer?
options.translation: Ah, I understand! And then, how do you cook it?
options.feedback: Good, you're moving on to the cooking process.

options.text: Então, é o alho e o louro que dão o sabor? E o sal grosso?
options.translation: So, it's the garlic and bay leaf that give the flavour? And the coarse salt?
options.feedback: Good follow-up, showing engagement with the details.

nodes.npc_text: Pois é! O sal grosso ajuda a temperar e a reter os sucos. E para a carne ficar tenra, é importante não cozer demais. Frita-se rápido.
nodes.npc_translation: That's right! The coarse salt helps season and retain the juices. And for the meat to be tender, it's important not to overcook it. You fry it quickly.

options.text: Frita-se? Pensei que era assado. Obrigado pela dica!
options.translation: You fry it? I thought it was roasted. Thanks for the tip!
options.feedback: Excellent, you've clarified a common misconception and thanked her.

options.text: Ah, entendi! Então, depois de marinar, frita-se?
options.translation: Ah, I understand! So, after marinating, you fry it?
options.feedback: Good, you're confirming the process.

nodes.npc_text: Sim, depois de marinar, espeta-se nos paus de louro e frita-se na brasa, ou numa chapa bem quente, um bocado de cada lado. É rápido!
nodes.npc_translation: Yes, after marinating, you skewer it on bay laurel sticks and fry it on the coals, or on a very hot griddle, a bit on each side. It's quick!

options.text: Muito obrigado, Dona Rosa! Vou seguir a sua receita. Um bom fim de semana!
options.translation: Thank you very much, Dona Rosa! I'm going to follow your recipe. Have a good weekend!
options.feedback: Perfect ending! You've thanked her and said goodbye.

options.text: Uhm... e os paus de louro, onde é que se arranjam?
options.translation: Uhm... and the bay laurel sticks, where do you get them?
options.feedback: A very Madeiran question! Good detail to ask about.

nodes.npc_text: De nada! É isso mesmo, frita-se para ficar suculenta. Depois diz-me como correu!
nodes.npc_translation: You're welcome! That's it, you fry it to make it juicy. Let me know how it goes!

options.text: Vou dizer, sim! Muito obrigado e um bom fim de semana!
options.translation: I will, yes! Thank you very much and have a good weekend!
options.feedback: Great interaction, you've ended politely.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, os paus de louro! Ou se têm em casa, ou se pede a um vizinho, ou então usa-se espetos de metal, mas não é a mesma coisa. Vai a uma loja de produtos regionais, talvez tenham!
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, the bay laurel sticks! Either you have them at home, or you ask a neighbour, or else you use metal skewers, but it's not the same thing. Go to a regional products shop, maybe they'll have them!

options.text: Ok, vou procurar! Muito obrigado por tudo, Dona Rosa!
options.translation: Okay, I'll look! Thank you very much for everything, Dona Rosa!
options.feedback: You've got all the info you need and thanked her.

mission.title: Ask for a Recipe
prep: Review the impersonal 'se' for giving instructions (e.g., 'amassa-se', 'deixa-se descansar').
Familiarize yourself with common cooking verbs and ingredients in Portuguese.
Practice asking 'Como é que se faz X?' and 'E depois, o que se faz?'
fallback_phrases: Um bocadinho mais devagar, por favor.
likely_responses: É um truque que aprendi com a minha avó.

goals: Understand and use the impersonal 'se' to describe cooking steps.
Ask and answer questions about ingredients and preparation methods for Madeiran dishes.
Engage in a natural conversation about food and recipes with a local speaker.

Say in Portuguese: It takes {ingredient1} and {ingredient2}.
Say in Portuguese: You {action_verb} {object}.
Say in Portuguese: And then, {question_phrase}?
Recall the Portuguese for: recipe
Recall the Portuguese for: to knead
Recall the Portuguese for: yeast
Recall the Portuguese for: to let rest

sit-d102-fish-market.title: At the Fish Market
sit-d102-fish-market.summary: The fish hall at the Mercado: naming the catch (espada, atum, bodião), asking what is fresh today, how much for a posta, and having it cleaned (amanhado) — fast counter talk.

variants.text: Quanto é que custa o quilo da {fish_type}?
variants.translation: How much does a kilo of {fish_type} cost?

vocabulary.note: A Madeiran specialty fish.
A common local fish in Madeira.
Diminutive, implying 'very' or 'nicely' fresh.
The main market in Funchal, famous for its fish hall.

dialogues.title: Buying Espada
dialogues.context: A customer approaches a fish stall in the vibrant fish hall of the Mercado dos Lavradores in Funchal.

lines.speaker: Peixeira
lines.text: Diga, minha senhora! O que deseja?
lines.translation: Hello, madam! What do you wish?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Bom dia! Tem espada fresquinha hoje?
lines.translation: Good morning! Do you have fresh scabbardfish today?

lines.speaker: Peixeira
lines.text: Temos sim! Acabadinha de chegar. Quer uma posta?
lines.translation: Yes, we do! Just arrived. Do you want a steak?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Sim, uma posta. Quanto é que custa o quilo?
lines.translation: Yes, one steak. How much is a kilo?

lines.speaker: Peixeira
lines.text: Está a doze euros o quilo. Quer que eu amanhe?
lines.translation: It's twelve euros a kilo. Do you want me to clean it?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Sim, por favor. Pode amanhar.
lines.translation: Yes, please. You can clean it.

lines.speaker: Peixeira
lines.text: P'ra já! Já fica pronto.
lines.translation: Right away! It'll be ready soon.

cultural_notes.title: The Mercado dos Lavradores Fish Hall
cultural_notes.body: The fish hall at Funchal's Mercado dos Lavradores is an iconic and lively place. It's common to see a wide variety of fresh fish, including the famous 'espada' (black scabbardfish), 'atum' (tuna), and 'bodião' (parrotfish). Vendors are often very vocal and keen to help, sometimes using informal but polite address like 'minha senhora' (my madam) or 'meu senhor' (my sir).

cultural_notes.title: Asking for Fish Cleaning ('Amanhar')
cultural_notes.body: When buying fish in Portugal, especially at markets, it's standard practice to ask the fishmonger to clean and prepare the fish for you. The verb 'amanhar' specifically refers to this process of gutting, scaling, and sometimes cutting the fish into steaks ('postas'). It's a free service and expected, so don't hesitate to ask: 'Pode amanhar?' or 'Quer que eu amanhe?' (Do you want me to clean it?) is a common question from the vendor.

roleplay.scenario: You are at a fish stall in the Mercado dos Lavradores. You want to buy some tuna (atum) for dinner.

nodes.npc_text: Olá! Posso ajudar?
nodes.npc_translation: Hello! Can I help?

options.text: Sim, queria atum, por favor.
options.translation: Yes, I'd like tuna, please.
options.feedback: Good start! You clearly stated what you wanted.

options.text: Está fresquinho hoje?
options.translation: Is it fresh today?
options.feedback: A good general question about freshness. Now, specify the fish.

nodes.npc_text: Atum, claro! Quer uma posta ou um bocado?
nodes.npc_translation: Tuna, of course! Do you want a steak or a piece?

options.text: Uma posta, se faz favor. Quanto custa?
options.translation: A steak, please. How much is it?
options.feedback: Excellent, you specified the cut and asked for the price.

options.text: Está fresquinho?
options.translation: Is it fresh?
options.feedback: Good to confirm freshness, even if you just asked generally.

nodes.npc_text: Todo o peixe aqui é fresquinho, minha senhora! Acabadinho de chegar.
nodes.npc_translation: All the fish here is fresh, madam! Just arrived.

options.text: Pois, mas queria atum. Tem?
options.translation: Right, but I wanted tuna. Do you have it?
options.feedback: Good, you acknowledged the vendor and redirected to your specific request.

nodes.npc_text: Sim, o atum está ótimo hoje! Acabadinho de chegar.
nodes.npc_translation: Yes, the tuna is great today! Just arrived.

options.text: Ótimo! Quanto é que custa a posta?
options.translation: Great! How much is a steak?
options.feedback: Perfect, you're moving towards the purchase.

nodes.npc_text: A posta está a dezasseis euros o quilo.
nodes.npc_translation: The steak is sixteen euros a kilo.

options.text: Pode amanhar, por favor?
options.translation: Can you clean it, please?
options.feedback: Asking for the cleaning service is very common and polite.

options.text: Vou levar só esta. Obrigada.
options.translation: I'll just take this one. Thank you.
options.feedback: You've completed your purchase without cleaning, which is also fine.

nodes.npc_text: P'ra já! Fica já pronto.
nodes.npc_translation: Right away! It'll be ready soon.

options.text: Muito obrigada!
options.translation: Thank you very much!
options.feedback: A polite way to end the interaction. Well done!

mission.title: Buy fish for dinner at the market

Practice asking 'Está fresquinho hoje?' to inquire about freshness.
Rehearse asking 'Quanto é que custa?' for prices.
Practice requesting the fish to be cleaned with 'Pode amanhar?'.
fallback_phrases: Não percebi, pode repetir, por favor?
Pode falar mais devagar, por favor?
likely_responses: É X euros o quilo/a posta.

goals: Ask for a specific type of fish at a market stall.
Inquire about the freshness of the fish.
Ask for the price of fish, specifying the unit (kilo/posta).
Request that the fish be cleaned and prepared.
Understand common phrases and responses from fishmongers in a market setting.

Say in Portuguese: Is it fresh today?
Say in Portuguese: Can you clean it (the fish)?
Say in Portuguese: How much does the {fish_type} cost?

Recall the Portuguese for: tuna

Recall the Portuguese for: fish steak / slice

sit-d103-coffee-culture.title: Coffee Culture: Chinesa, Garoto & Bica
sit-d103-coffee-culture.summary: Madeira’s coffee code: chinesa, garoto, bica, café duplo — what each is, how to order yours, café etiquette at the counter versus the table, and paying at the till.

variants.note: More direct and common at a busy counter.

phrase_patterns.base: É p'ra beber aqui ou p'ra levar?
phrase_patterns.translation: Is it to drink here or to take away?

vocabulary.note: Common term for espresso in Lisbon and Madeira.
Specific to Madeira for a latte/coffee with milk, similar to 'galão' elsewhere in Portugal.

vocabulary.translation: espresso with a dash of milk
vocabulary.note: Espresso with a little bit of milk foam.

dialogues.title: Ordering at the counter
dialogues.context: At a local café in Funchal, a customer orders coffee from the barista.

lines.speaker: Empregado
lines.text: Diga! O que vai ser?
lines.translation: Tell me! What will it be?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Queria uma chinesa, por favor. E um bolo do caco.
lines.translation: I'd like a latte, please. And a bolo do caco.

lines.speaker: Empregado
lines.text: Tá bem. É p'ra beber aqui, no balcão?
lines.translation: Alright. Is it to drink here, at the counter?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Sim, sim. Aqui mesmo.
lines.translation: Yes, yes. Right here.

lines.speaker: Empregado
lines.text: P'ra já!
lines.translation: Coming right up!

dialogues.title: Coffee advice from a neighbor
dialogues.context: A learner is talking to a neighbor about ordering coffee in Madeira.

lines.speaker: Vizinha
lines.text: Então, já sabe o que pedir no café?
lines.translation: So, do you know what to order at the café yet?

lines.speaker: Aprendiz
lines.text: Ainda não sei bem a diferença entre uma bica e um garoto.
lines.translation: I don't quite know the difference between an espresso and a garoto yet.

lines.speaker: Vizinha
lines.text: Ah, é fácil! A bica é só café, o garoto é com um bocadinho de leite. E a chinesa é como um galão, mais leite.
lines.translation: Ah, it's easy! The bica is just coffee, the garoto is with a little bit of milk. And the chinesa is like a galão, more milk.

lines.speaker: Aprendiz
lines.text: Pois, faz sentido. Obrigada pela dica!
lines.translation: Right, that makes sense. Thanks for the tip!

cultural_notes.title: Madeira's Coffee Code
cultural_notes.body: In Madeira, ordering coffee has its own specific terms. A 'bica' is a standard espresso. A 'garoto' is an espresso with a small amount of milk, usually just a dash of foam. The 'chinesa' is a local term for a latte (coffee with a larger amount of milk), similar to what's called a 'galão' in mainland Portugal. A 'café duplo' is a double espresso. Knowing these terms will help you order exactly what you want like a local!

cultural_notes.title: Counter vs. Table Service & Paying
cultural_notes.body: It's common to drink coffee standing at the 'balcão' (counter) in cafés, which is usually cheaper than sitting at a table. If you sit at a table, an 'empregado' will serve you. When you're ready to pay, especially at the counter, you usually go to the 'caixa' (till) and say 'Posso pagar?' or 'Quanto é?'. It's rare for the barista to collect payment directly at a busy counter.

roleplay.scenario: You're at a busy café in Funchal. You want to order a coffee and pay.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia! O que vai ser?
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning! What will it be?

options.text: Queria uma bica, por favor.
options.translation: I'd like an espresso, please.
options.feedback: Great start, polite and clear!

options.text: Uma chinesa, p'ra já!
options.translation: A latte, right away!
options.feedback: Direct and confident, perfect for a busy counter!

nodes.npc_text: Uma bica. É p'ra beber aqui ou p'ra levar?
nodes.npc_translation: An espresso. Is it to drink here or to take away?

options.text: É p'ra beber aqui.
options.translation: It's to drink here.
options.feedback: Correctly answering the common question.

options.text: P'ra levar, por favor.
options.translation: To take away, please.
options.feedback: Good, indicating you want it to go.

nodes.npc_text: Uma chinesa. No balcão?
nodes.npc_translation: A latte. At the counter?

options.text: Sim, no balcão.
options.translation: Yes, at the counter.
options.feedback: Confirming your choice correctly.

options.text: Não, numa mesa, por favor.
options.translation: No, at a table, please.
options.feedback: Good, asking for table service.

nodes.npc_text: P'ra já! Fica a pagar na caixa quando acabar.
nodes.npc_translation: Coming right up! You pay at the till when you're done.

options.text: Muito obrigada!
options.translation: Thank you very much!
options.feedback: Polite acknowledgment. Now, time to pay!

nodes.npc_text: P'ra levar. São dois euros. Pode pagar aqui.
nodes.npc_translation: To take away. It's two euros. You can pay here.

options.text: Aqui tem.
options.translation: Here you go.
options.feedback: Successfully paid and concluded the interaction.

nodes.npc_text: Claro. Pode sentar-se onde quiser. Já levo.
nodes.npc_translation: Of course. You can sit wherever you like. I'll bring it.

options.text: Obrigada.
options.translation: Thank you.
options.feedback: You've successfully secured a table. The payment will likely happen at the table after you finish.

nodes.npc_text: Diga! Já pagou?
nodes.npc_translation: Tell me! Have you paid?

options.text: Ainda não. Posso pagar agora?
options.translation: Not yet. Can I pay now?
options.feedback: Good, asking to pay at the till.

options.text: Quanto é, por favor?
options.translation: How much is it, please?
options.feedback: Directly asking for the price, also good.

nodes.npc_text: Claro. É um euro e vinte. Quer fatura?
nodes.npc_translation: Of course. It's one euro twenty. Do you want a receipt?

options.text: Não, obrigada. Aqui tem.
options.translation: No, thank you. Here you go.
options.feedback: You've successfully completed your coffee order and payment!

options.text: Sim, por favor.
options.translation: Yes, please.
options.feedback: You've successfully completed your coffee order and payment, asking for a receipt.

Rehearse 'É p'ra beber aqui ou p'ra levar?' and how to answer.
Practice asking 'Posso pagar?' or 'Quanto é?' at the till.

Understand and respond to questions about drinking 'here or to go'.
Ask to pay and understand the price at a café.

Say in Portuguese: Is it to drink here or to take away?
review_items.answer: É p'ra beber aqui ou p'ra levar?

Say in Portuguese: How much is it?

Recall the Portuguese for: latte
Recall the Portuguese for: espresso with a dash of milk
Recall the Portuguese for: double espresso

sit-d104-table-etiquette.title: Compliments & Refusals at the Table
sit-d104-table-etiquette.summary: Being fed by Madeirans: complimenting the food properly, accepting seconds, refusing thirds without offending (não posso mais, estava ótimo), and offering to help.

variants.text: Sim, por favor, só um bocadinho.
variants.translation: Yes, please, just a little bit.

phrase_patterns.translation: No, {thank_you}, I can't eat any more.

variants.text: Estava ótimo, mas já estou satisfeito/a.
variants.translation: It was great, but I'm already full.
variants.note: Use 'satisfeito' for male, 'satisfeita' for female.

variants.text: Não, não, já não posso mais. Estava divinal!
variants.translation: No, no, I can't anymore. It was divine!

phrase_patterns.translation: Do you want me to help {action}?

variants.text: Precisa de ajuda com alguma coisa?
variants.translation: Do you need help with anything?

vocabulary.note: Common in Portugal, especially Madeira, for 'a little bit'.
Madeiran regionalism for 'batata'.
Traditional Madeiran alcoholic drink.

dialogues.title: Sunday Lunch Compliments
dialogues.context: At Dona Maria's house for Sunday lunch. She's serving a traditional Madeiran dish.

lines.speaker: Dona Maria
lines.text: Então, come mais um bocadinho, rapaz. Está bom?
lines.translation: So, eat a bit more, young man. Is it good?

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Dona Maria, está delicioso! Os meus parabéns. Posso comer mais um bocadinho, sim.
lines.translation: Dona Maria, it's delicious! My compliments. Yes, I can eat a little more.

lines.speaker: Dona Maria
lines.text: Ai, que bom! Ainda há mais carne e semilhas. Não fiques com vergonha.
lines.translation: Oh, that's good! There's still more meat and potatoes. Don't be shy.

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Pois, pois. Só um pouco mais de semilha, se faz favor. Está mesmo no ponto.
lines.translation: Yes, yes. Just a little more potato, please. It's perfectly cooked.

dialogues.title: Polite Refusal and Offer to Help
dialogues.context: Later in the same lunch, Dona Maria is offering dessert, and João is full.

lines.speaker: Dona Maria
lines.text: Agora, uma fatiazinha de bolo de mel? É caseiro, sabe.
lines.translation: Now, a little slice of honey cake? It's homemade, you know.

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Ai, Dona Maria, muito obrigado, mas não consigo mesmo comer mais nada. Estava tudo divinal!
lines.translation: Oh, Dona Maria, thank you very much, but I really can't eat anything else. Everything was divine!

lines.speaker: Dona Maria
lines.text: Tem a certeza? É uma fatia pequena...
lines.translation: Are you sure? It's a small slice...

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Tenho, tenho! Já estou satisfeito. Mas quer que eu ajude a tirar a mesa ou a lavar a loiça?
lines.translation: Yes, I am! I'm already full. But do you want me to help clear the table or wash the dishes?

lines.speaker: Dona Maria
lines.text: Não, não, vá descansar. Fica para mim. Mas obrigado pela oferta, rapaz.
lines.translation: No, no, go rest. I'll do it. But thanks for the offer, young man.

cultural_notes.title: Complimenting the Cook
cultural_notes.body: In Madeira, as in most of Portugal, it's very important to compliment the host's cooking. Phrases like 'Está delicioso!' or 'Os meus parabéns!' are expected and show appreciation. Even if you're not a fan, find something positive to say. Sincerity is valued.

cultural_notes.title: Refusing Food Politely
cultural_notes.body: It's common for hosts to offer more food multiple times. To refuse politely without offending, state firmly that you are full ('já estou satisfeito/a' or 'não consigo comer mais') and reiterate how good the food was ('estava ótimo', 'estava divinal'). Offering to help clear the table or wash dishes is a good gesture that shows your gratitude and respect.

roleplay.scenario: You are having dinner at your neighbour, Dona Fátima's, house. She's a lovely older lady who loves to feed people.

nodes.npc_text: Então, já comeu? Está bom o peixe-espada preto com banana?
nodes.npc_translation: So, have you eaten? Is the black scabbard fish with banana good?

options.text: Sim, está ótimo, Dona Fátima! Os meus parabéns!
options.translation: Yes, it's great, Dona Fátima! My compliments!
options.feedback: Perfect! You've complimented the food and given your compliments to the cook.

options.text: Está bom.
options.translation: It's good.
options.feedback: It's okay, but a bit short. Try to be more enthusiastic and use a stronger adjective.

nodes.npc_text: Ai, que bom que gostou! Quer mais um bocadinho de peixe? Ou umas semilhas?
nodes.npc_translation: Oh, good that you liked it! Do you want a little more fish? Or some potatoes?

options.text: Sim, por favor, só um bocadinho mais de semilhas.
options.translation: Yes, please, just a little more potatoes.
options.feedback: Excellent! You've accepted seconds politely and specified what you want.

options.text: Não, obrigado/a.
options.translation: No, thank you.
options.feedback: This is a bit abrupt. In Portugal, it's polite to give a reason or compliment again when refusing.

nodes.npc_text: Só bom? Não está delicioso? Eu fiz com tanto carinho...
nodes.npc_translation: Only good? Isn't it delicious? I made it with so much care...

options.text: Desculpe, Dona Fátima! Está delicioso, sim! Os meus parabéns!
options.translation: Sorry, Dona Fátima! It is delicious, yes! My compliments!
options.feedback: Good recovery! You've corrected your politeness.

nodes.npc_text: Não quer mais nada? Tem a certeza? Ainda há tanto!
nodes.npc_translation: You don't want anything else? Are you sure? There's still so much!

options.text: Não, muito obrigado/a, Dona Fátima. Estava tudo ótimo, mas já estou satisfeito/a.
options.translation: No, thank you very much, Dona Fátima. Everything was great, but I'm already full.
options.feedback: Much better! You've refused politely and explained why, while still complimenting the food.

nodes.npc_text: Pronto, aqui está! Mais um bocadinho para ficar forte. E para a sobremesa, um pudim de maracujá?
nodes.npc_translation: There you go! A little more to get strong. And for dessert, a passion fruit pudding?

options.text: Ai, Dona Fátima, muito obrigado/a, mas não consigo comer mais nada. Estava tudo divinal! Quer que eu ajude a tirar a mesa?
options.translation: Oh, Dona Fátima, thank you very much, but I really can't eat anything else. Everything was divine! Do you want me to help clear the table?
options.feedback: Excellent! You've politely refused dessert, complimented the meal again, and offered to help. This is a perfect exit!

options.text: Sim, quero um pudim.
options.translation: Yes, I want a pudding.
options.feedback: You can accept, but be aware that in Madeira, hosts might keep offering until you're truly bursting! A polite refusal is often expected at the third offer.

nodes.npc_text: Pois é, percebo. Mas não quer nem um café? Ou uma poncha?
nodes.npc_translation: Yes, I understand. But you don't want even a coffee? Or a poncha?

options.text: Um café, sim, por favor. E quer que eu ajude a tirar a mesa?
options.translation: A coffee, yes, please. And do you want me to help clear the table?
options.feedback: Very good! You've accepted a smaller item (coffee) and still offered help, showing gratitude.

options.text: Não, obrigado/a por tudo. Tenho de ir.
options.translation: No, thank you for everything. I have to go.
options.feedback: This is a bit abrupt to leave immediately after refusing. A coffee or offering to help is usually a good transition.

mission.title: The Neighbour's Dinner
prep: Review phrases for complimenting food, like 'Está delicioso!' or 'Os meus parabéns!'
Practice accepting seconds politely: 'Só um bocadinho mais, se faz favor.'
Rehearse polite refusals for thirds, emphasizing you're full but it was excellent: 'Não consigo comer mais, estava ótimo!'
Practice offering to help with chores: 'Quer que eu ajude a tirar a mesa?'
fallback_phrases: Um bocadinho mais devagar, se faz favor.
likely_responses: Não é preciso, fica para mim.

goals: Compliment the host's cooking sincerely.
Accept a second helping politely.
Refuse further offers of food without offending.
Offer to help with clearing the table or washing dishes.

Say in Portuguese: No, {thank_you}, I can't eat any more.
Say in Portuguese: Do you want me to help {action}?

sit-d105-week-15-stress-test.summary: A full food day: the fish market in the morning, coffee at the counter, espetada dinner with hosts — ordering, food talk and table social code combined.

phrase_patterns.base: Então, como é que correu o {event}?

variants.note: Common indirect way to order

vocabulary.note: A very popular fish in Madeira
Madeiran regionalism for 'batata'
Common pt-PT term for espresso
pt-PT for 'açougue'
