dialogues.title: At the Fish Market
dialogues.context: You're at the Mercado dos Lavradores in Funchal, buying fish for dinner. It's busy.

lines.speaker: Vendedor
lines.text: Diga, o que vai ser? Temos espada fresquinha!
lines.translation: Tell me, what will it be? We have very fresh scabbard fish!

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: Bom dia! Queria meio quilo de espada, se faz favor.
lines.translation: Good morning! I'd like half a kilo of scabbard fish, please.

lines.speaker: Vendedor
lines.text: Meio quilo de espada. Quer mais alguma coisa? Atum, talvez?
lines.translation: Half a kilo of scabbard fish. Do you want anything else? Tuna, perhaps?

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: Não, obrigada. É só isso. Quanto é?
lines.translation: No, thank you. That's all. How much is it?

lines.speaker: Vendedor
lines.text: São sete euros e vinte e cinco. P'ra já!
lines.translation: That's seven euros and twenty-five. Here you go!

dialogues.title: Espetada Dinner with Friends
dialogues.context: You're at your Madeiran friends' (Ana and João) house for a traditional espetada dinner.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Então, já provaste a espetada? O João é que a fez!
lines.translation: So, have you tried the espetada yet? João made it!

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: Sim, já sim! Está uma delícia, João. Parabéns!
lines.translation: Yes, I have! It's delicious, João. Congratulations!

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Pois é, fico contente que gostes. E as semilhas? Estão boas?
lines.translation: Indeed, I'm glad you like it. And the potatoes? Are they good?

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: Estão ótimas! Adoro semilhas assadas assim.
lines.translation: They're great! I love roasted potatoes like this.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Queres mais um copo de vinho? É da casa, do meu pai.
lines.translation: Do you want another glass of wine? It's homemade, from my father.

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: Ah, sim, por favor! Obrigada, Ana.
lines.translation: Oh, yes, please! Thank you, Ana.

cultural_notes.title: Addressing Vendors and Service Staff
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, it's common and polite to address vendors or service staff with 'o senhor' (the gentleman) or 'a senhora' (the lady) even when not explicitly using their name. Phrases like 'Queria... se faz favor' (I'd like... please) are more common than 'Eu quero...' (I want...). In busy markets, short, direct requests are fine, but always with 'se faz favor' or 'por favor'.

cultural_notes.title: Complimenting Food at a Host's Home
cultural_notes.body: When invited to someone's home for a meal, it's very important to compliment the food. Use phrases like 'Está ótimo/a!', 'Está uma delícia!', or 'Está uma maravilha!'. It shows appreciation for their effort and hospitality. Hosts will often ask if you like specific dishes, giving you a chance to praise them.

roleplay.scenario: You're having coffee at a local café counter in the morning. The barista is busy but friendly. You want to order a coffee and maybe ask about a local pastry.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia! Diz, o que vai ser?
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning! Tell me, what will it be?

options.text: Bom dia! Queria uma bica, se faz favor.
options.translation: Good morning! I'd like an espresso, please.
options.feedback: Excellent, polite and direct. 'Bica' is perfect for espresso.

options.text: Uma bica.
options.translation: An espresso.
options.feedback: A bit too direct. Adding 'se faz favor' or 'por favor' makes it much more polite.

nodes.npc_text: P'ra já! Quer mais alguma coisa? Um bolo do caco, talvez?
nodes.npc_translation: Right away! Do you want anything else? A bolo do caco, perhaps?

options.text: Sim, um bolo do caco, por favor.
options.translation: Yes, a bolo do caco, please.
options.feedback: Great, a good choice and polite.

options.text: Não, obrigada, é só a bica.
options.translation: No, thank you, just the espresso.
options.feedback: Perfectly fine to decline politely.

nodes.npc_text: Ok, uma bica e um bolo do caco. São dois euros e cinquenta.
nodes.npc_translation: Okay, an espresso and a bolo do caco. That's two euros and fifty cents.

options.text: Aqui tem. Obrigada!
options.translation: Here you go. Thank you!
options.feedback: Polite and natural ending to the transaction.

nodes.npc_text: Ok, então só a bica. É um euro e vinte.
nodes.npc_translation: Okay, just the espresso then. That's one euro and twenty cents.

options.text: Aqui tem. Obrigada e bom trabalho!
options.translation: Here you go. Thank you and have a good day!
options.feedback: A very nice touch to wish them a good day/work day.

sit-d106-playing-the-local-guide.title: Playing the Local Guide
sit-d106-playing-the-local-guide.summary: Friends are visiting and a Madeiran asks what you will show them: explaining Madeira in Portuguese — where to take visitors, what to skip, and taking recommendations.

phrase_patterns.base: Então, o que é que vais mostrar aos teus amigos?
phrase_patterns.translation: So, what are you going to show your friends?

variants.text: O que é que planeias mostrar aos teus amigos?
variants.translation: What do you plan to show your friends?

phrase_patterns.base: Acho que não vale a pena ir a {place_to_skip}.
phrase_patterns.translation: I don't think it's worth going to {place_to_skip}.

options: o Jardim Botânico (se tiverem pouco tempo)

vocabulary.note: Specific to Madeira
Specific to Madeira
Typical Madeiran dish, usually beef on a laurel stick
Madeiran bread, often served with garlic butter
Madeiran reduction of 'para já'

dialogues.title: Showing Friends Around
dialogues.context: A vizinha Fátima encontra o/a aluno/a no mercado e pergunta sobre os amigos que estão a visitar a Madeira.

lines.speaker: Fátima (Vizinha)
lines.text: Então, os teus amigos já chegaram? O que é que lhes vais mostrar cá na Madeira?
lines.translation: So, your friends have arrived? What are you going to show them here in Madeira?

lines.speaker: Aluno/a
lines.text: Sim, chegaram ontem! Estava a pensar levá-los à Ponta de São Lourenço e a uma levada.
lines.translation: Yes, they arrived yesterday! I was thinking of taking them to Ponta de São Lourenço and to a levada.

lines.speaker: Fátima (Vizinha)
lines.text: Ah, boa ideia! A Ponta é linda. Mas qual levada? Há umas que são muito cheias ou difíceis.
lines.translation: Ah, good idea! The Ponta is beautiful. But which levada? There are some that are very crowded or difficult.

lines.speaker: Aluno/a
lines.text: Pois, estava a pensar na Levada dos Cedros, mas dizem que é um bocado puxada.
lines.translation: Well, I was thinking of Levada dos Cedros, but they say it's a bit tough.

lines.speaker: Fátima (Vizinha)
lines.text: É, é um bocado. P'ra algo mais fácil e bonito, podiam ir à Levada do Rei. E não se esqueçam de provar a poncha!
lines.translation: Yes, it is a bit. For something easier and beautiful, you could go to Levada do Rei. And don't forget to try the poncha!

lines.speaker: Aluno/a
lines.text: Claro! E o bolo do caco. Achas que vale a pena ir ao Jardim Tropical?
lines.translation: Of course! And bolo do caco. Do you think it's worth going to the Tropical Garden?

lines.speaker: Fátima (Vizinha)
lines.text: Hum, é bonito, mas se o tempo for curto, acho que não. Há miradouros mais espetaculares para ver.
lines.translation: Hmm, it's beautiful, but if time is short, I don't think so. There are more spectacular viewpoints to see.

lines.speaker: Aluno/a
lines.text: Pois é. Obrigada pela dica!
lines.translation: That's true. Thanks for the tip!

lines.speaker: Fátima (Vizinha)
lines.text: De nada! Diverte-te a ser guia!
lines.translation: You're welcome! Have fun being a guide!

cultural_notes.title: Madeiran Hospitality and Local Advice
cultural_notes.body: In Madeira, as in much of Portugal, it's common for neighbours and friends to offer advice, even if not explicitly asked. This is a sign of care and community. When someone asks about your plans, they are often genuinely interested and happy to share their local knowledge. Phrases like 'Pois é' (That's true) or 'Obrigada pela dica' (Thanks for the tip) are good ways to acknowledge and appreciate their input without necessarily committing to follow it.

cultural_notes.title: Must-Try Madeiran Experiences
cultural_notes.body: When guiding visitors, highlighting local food, drinks, and unique landscapes is key. Mentioning 'poncha', 'espetada', 'bolo do caco', 'levadas', and 'miradouros' shows that you're embracing the Madeiran culture. These are not just tourist attractions but integral parts of daily life and identity on the island.

roleplay.scenario: You're at a local café, enjoying a 'bica' (espresso) when your friend, Miguel, spots you. He knows you have friends visiting and asks about your plans to show them around Madeira.

nodes.npc_text: Olá! Então, os teus amigos já cá estão, certo? O que é que lhes vais mostrar cá na ilha?
nodes.npc_translation: Hi! So, your friends are here, right? What are you going to show them here on the island?

options.text: Estava a pensar levá-los à Ponta de São Lourenço e depois a uma levada.
options.translation: I was thinking of taking them to Ponta de São Lourenço and then to a levada.
options.feedback: Good start! You're suggesting popular Madeiran spots.

options.text: Ainda não sei bem. Tens alguma sugestão para quem visita pela primeira vez?
options.translation: I don't know yet. Do you have any suggestions for first-time visitors?
options.feedback: Great way to ask for recommendations and engage your friend.

nodes.npc_text: A Ponta de São Lourenço é fantástica para as vistas! Que levada é que tens em mente? Há umas que são mais exigentes que outras.
nodes.npc_translation: Ponta de São Lourenço is fantastic for the views! Which levada do you have in mind? Some are more demanding than others.

options.text: Estava a pensar na Levada das 25 Fontes. É bonita, não é?
options.translation: I was thinking of Levada das 25 Fontes. It's beautiful, isn't it?
options.feedback: Good choice, a well-known levada. Now see what Miguel thinks.

options.text: Ainda não decidimos a levada. Tens alguma para iniciantes?
options.translation: We haven't decided on the levada yet. Do you have one for beginners?
options.feedback: Asking for a specific type of recommendation is very practical.

nodes.npc_text: Claro! Para quem vem pela primeira vez, o Mercado dos Lavradores é um sítio muito interessante para ver as frutas. E não se esqueçam da poncha!
nodes.npc_translation: Of course! For first-timers, the Mercado dos Lavradores is a very interesting place to see the fruits. And don't forget the poncha!

options.text: Ah, sim, o mercado é uma ótima ideia! E onde é que podemos provar uma boa poncha?
options.translation: Ah, yes, the market is a great idea! And where can we try a good poncha?
options.feedback: Excellent, you're following up on his suggestions and asking for more details.

nodes.npc_text: Para iniciantes, a Levada dos Balcões é perfeita. É curta, fácil e a vista no final é incrível! É um sítio bonito para tirar fotos.
nodes.npc_translation: For beginners, Levada dos Balcões is perfect. It's short, easy, and the view at the end is incredible! It's a beautiful place to take photos.

options.text: Parece perfeito! E depois da levada, onde podemos almoçar uma espetada?
options.translation: Sounds perfect! And after the levada, where can we have espetada for lunch?
options.feedback: Good, you're planning the next step and asking for another local food recommendation.

nodes.npc_text: A Levada das 25 Fontes é linda, mas prepara-te para muita gente! É um bocado turística. Se eles gostam de algo mais calmo, talvez outra seja melhor.
nodes.npc_translation: Levada das 25 Fontes is beautiful, but be prepared for a lot of people! It's a bit touristy. If they like something calmer, maybe another one would be better.

options.text: Pois é, não quero que seja demasiado cheia. Que outra levada sugeres, então?
options.translation: That's true, I don't want it to be too crowded. Which other levada do you suggest, then?
options.feedback: You've acknowledged his point and asked for an alternative, showing good conversational flow.

options.text: Ah, eles não se importam com a multidão. O importante é a paisagem!
options.translation: Oh, they don't mind the crowd. The important thing is the landscape!
options.feedback: You've made your decision clear. The conversation ends naturally here.

nodes.npc_text: Para poncha, o 'Pé de Cabra' é um clássico. Para espetada, o 'Abrigo do Pastor' é excelente, mas é um bocado longe. No Funchal, a 'Venda do Belo' também é boa.
nodes.npc_translation: For poncha, 'Pé de Cabra' is a classic. For espetada, 'Abrigo do Pastor' is excellent, but it's a bit far. In Funchal, 'Venda do Belo' is also good.

options.text: Ótimas dicas! Vou ver qual se encaixa melhor nos nossos planos. Obrigada!
options.translation: Great tips! I'll see which one fits our plans best. Thank you!
options.feedback: You've thanked your friend and ended the conversation politely. Well done!

sit-d107-microclimates-north-south.title: Microclimates & the North/South Divide
sit-d107-microclimates-north-south.summary: Island geography as conversation: sun in the south, rain in the north, the capacete, driving through four seasons in an hour — and the gentle north-versus-south teasing.

phrase_patterns.base: A serra está com o capacete.
phrase_patterns.translation: The mountains have the helmet (are covered in fog/clouds).

variants.text: Hoje a serra está com o capacete.
variants.translation: Today the mountains have the helmet.

variants.text: Olha, a serra está com o capacete de novo.
variants.translation: Look, the mountains have the helmet again.

phrase_patterns.translation: We go through {conditions} in an hour.

slots.name: climate_type
slots.description: A type of climate or weather

vocabulary.translation: helmet (local idiom for clouds/fog on mountains)
vocabulary.note: Madeiran regionalism

dialogues.title: Weather talk with neighbours
dialogues.context: Two neighbours, Maria and João, are chatting outside their homes in Funchal about the day's weather.

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Bom dia, João! Que tempo estranho, não é? Aqui no Funchal está sol, mas lá para o norte deve estar a chover.
lines.translation: Good morning, João! What strange weather, isn't it? Here in Funchal it's sunny, but up north it must be raining.

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Pois é, Maria! Vim de lá agora e passei por tudo: sol, nevoeiro, até umas pingas. A serra está mesmo com o capacete hoje.
lines.translation: Indeed, Maria! I just came from there and went through everything: sun, fog, even a few drops. The mountains really have the helmet today.

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Ah, o nosso microclima! É sempre assim. Mas aqui no sul é que é bom, não é?
lines.translation: Ah, our microclimate! It's always like that. But here in the south is where it's good, isn't it?

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Claro! O sul é sempre o sul. Não troco o nosso sol por nada!
lines.translation: Of course! The south is always the south. I wouldn't trade our sun for anything!

cultural_notes.title: The 'Capacete' of Madeira
cultural_notes.body: In Madeira, the word 'capacete' (helmet) is often used colloquially to describe the phenomenon when the central mountain range (a serra) is covered in a thick layer of clouds or fog. This 'helmet' typically brings cooler, wetter weather to the north side of the island, while the south remains sunny and warm. It's a common topic of conversation and a clear indicator of the island's unique microclimates.

cultural_notes.title: North vs. South Teasing
cultural_notes.body: Due to the distinct microclimates, there's a playful rivalry and teasing between residents of the north and south of the island. The south is generally sunnier and warmer, while the north is greener and often wetter. Locals might jokingly boast about their region's weather or gently tease others about the 'bad weather' on the other side. This is a sign of local belonging and should be understood as good-natured banter.

roleplay.scenario: You're at the local market in Funchal, buying fresh fruit from Senhora Fátima, a friendly vendor. The weather is a common topic of conversation.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia, minha querida! Que tempo tão bonito hoje, não é? Aqui no Funchal está uma maravilha!
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning, my dear! What beautiful weather today, isn't it? Here in Funchal it's wonderful!

options.text: Sim, está ótimo! Mas no norte deve ser diferente.
options.translation: Yes, it's great! But in the north it must be different.
options.feedback: Good observation, showing awareness of the island's microclimates.

options.text: Está sim! E como está o tempo lá para a sua zona?
options.translation: It is! And how's the weather up in your area?
options.feedback: A polite way to engage and learn more about her local area's weather.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, no norte? Pois é, lá é sempre uma incógnita. Às vezes, a serra está com o capacete e lá em cima está tudo nublado.
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, in the north? Well, it's always an unknown there. Sometimes, the mountains have the helmet and up there it's all cloudy.

options.text: É verdade! Já passei por sol e nevoeiro na mesma viagem.
options.translation: It's true! I've already experienced sun and fog on the same trip.
options.feedback: Relating your own experience reinforces the conversation.

options.text: Então, prefere o tempo aqui no sul?
options.translation: So, you prefer the weather here in the south?
options.feedback: A good way to prompt her opinion and engage in the 'north vs. south' dynamic.

nodes.npc_text: Eu sou da Ponta do Sol, e lá é quase sempre sol! É um microclima fabuloso. Mas se for à serra, pode apanhar o capacete num instante.
nodes.npc_translation: I'm from Ponta do Sol, and there it's almost always sunny! It's a fabulous microclimate. But if you go to the mountains, you can catch the helmet in an instant.

options.text: Que bom! O sol da Ponta do Sol é famoso.
options.translation: How nice! The sun of Ponta do Sol is famous.
options.feedback: A positive affirmation shows you're listening and appreciate her input.

options.text: Sim, o capacete é uma coisa típica da ilha, não é?
options.translation: Yes, the 'helmet' is a typical thing on the island, isn't it?
options.feedback: Showing understanding of the local idiom is great.

nodes.npc_text: Pois é! É a beleza da nossa ilha. Passamos por quatro estações numa hora, às vezes! É o que a torna tão especial.
nodes.npc_translation: Indeed! It's the beauty of our island. We go through four seasons in an hour, sometimes! That's what makes it so special.

options.text: Concordo plenamente. Obrigado pela conversa, Senhora Fátima!
options.translation: I completely agree. Thanks for the chat, Senhora Fátima!
options.feedback: A polite and natural way to end the conversation.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, claro! O sul é mais para o meu gosto, com o sol e o mar. Mas o norte tem a sua beleza, com todo aquele verde. É uma questão de preferência.
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, of course! The south is more to my liking, with the sun and the sea. But the north has its beauty, with all that green. It's a matter of preference.

options.text: Compreendo. É bom ter opções na mesma ilha!
options.translation: I understand. It's good to have options on the same island!
options.feedback: A diplomatic and appreciative response.

options.text: Pois é, cada um tem o seu encanto. Obrigado!
options.translation: Indeed, each has its charm. Thank you!
options.feedback: A simple and friendly way to conclude.

nodes.npc_text: Sim, é uma bênção! Volte sempre, minha querida.
nodes.npc_translation: Yes, it's a blessing! Come back always, my dear.

nodes.npc_text: É sim! É a nossa marca registada. Faz parte da paisagem e do tempo aqui na Madeira.
nodes.npc_translation: It is! It's our trademark. It's part of the landscape and the weather here in Madeira.

options.text: Muito interessante! Obrigado pela explicação.
options.translation: Very interesting! Thanks for the explanation.
options.feedback: Expressing gratitude is always good.

mission.title: Conversa sobre o tempo e a ilha
prep: Listen for comments about the weather, especially if it mentions different parts of the island.
Try to use the phrase 'a serra está com o capacete' if the mountains are cloudy.
Mention the difference between the north and south weather to a local.
Be prepared to express a preference for one side of the island (even if jokingly).
fallback_phrases: Pois é, o tempo aqui muda muito.
É sempre uma surpresa, não é?
likely_responses: Aqui no sul é que é bom!

goals: Discuss the distinct microclimates of Madeira.
Understand and correctly use the Madeiran idiom 'o capacete' in context.
Engage in lighthearted conversation about the north vs. south weather divide.
Share personal observations about weather changes on the island.

review_items.prompt: Say in Portuguese: In {location} it's {weather}, but in {other_location} it's {other_weather}.
Say in Portuguese: The mountains have the helmet (are covered in fog/clouds).
review_items.answer: A serra está com o capacete.

review_items.prompt: Say in Portuguese: We go through {conditions} in an hour.

sit-d108-boats-and-whales.title: Boats, Whales & the Sea Economy
sit-d108-boats-and-whales.summary: The working sea: whale-watching boats, the old whaling history, fishermen’s talk in Câmara de Lobos, and asking about going out on the water.

variants.translation: Are there excursions to see whales?

phrase_patterns.base: Os pescadores daqui ainda usam {este tipo de barco}?
phrase_patterns.translation: Do the fishermen here still use {this type of boat}?

variants.text: A pesca tradicional ainda é forte aqui?
variants.translation: Is traditional fishing still strong here?

phrase_patterns.base: Qual é a melhor altura p'ra {fazer} um {passeio de barco}?
phrase_patterns.translation: What's the best time to {do} a {boat trip}?

variants.text: Quando é que a visibilidade é melhor para o mar?
variants.translation: When is the visibility best for the sea?

phrase_patterns.base: A história da {caça à baleia} ainda é lembrada?
phrase_patterns.translation: Is the history of {whaling} still remembered?

vocabulary.note: Refers to the historical practice, now replaced by whale watching.

dialogues.title: At the Câmara de Lobos harbour
dialogues.context: The learner is talking to a local fisherman in Câmara de Lobos, asking about the sea.

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: Bom dia! Há muitos barcos a sair para o mar hoje?
lines.translation: Good morning! Are many boats going out to sea today?

lines.speaker: Pescador
lines.text: Bom dia! Pois é, hoje o tempo tá bom, então sim, alguns já foram.
lines.translation: Good morning! Yes, the weather is good today, so yes, some have already gone.

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: E há barcos que vão ver baleias ou golfinhos?
lines.translation: And are there boats that go to see whales or dolphins?

lines.speaker: Pescador
lines.text: Sim, há sempre! Principalmente da marina do Funchal. Aqui é mais pesca.
lines.translation: Yes, always! Mainly from Funchal marina. Here it's more fishing.

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: Percebo. A pesca ainda é muito importante para a vila?
lines.translation: I understand. Is fishing still very important for the village?

lines.speaker: Pescador
lines.text: Claro que sim! É a nossa vida, não é? E o peixe daqui é fresco, do melhor!
lines.translation: Of course! It's our life, isn't it? And the fish from here is fresh, the best!

cultural_notes.title: From Whaling to Whale Watching
cultural_notes.body: Madeira has a complex history with the sea. Until 1981, whaling was a significant industry, particularly in Caniçal. Today, the island is a prime destination for whale and dolphin watching, with strict regulations to protect marine life. Locals are proud of this shift, highlighting the preservation of nature while still valuing their historical connection to the sea.

cultural_notes.title: Câmara de Lobos - A Fishing Village
cultural_notes.body: Câmara de Lobos remains a vibrant fishing village, famous for its traditional boats and its unique atmosphere. While tourism has grown, fishing is still central to its identity and economy. Locals are often proud to share stories about their fishing heritage, and it's common to see fishermen mending nets or preparing their boats in the harbour.

roleplay.scenario: You are at a small kiosk near the port in Funchal, wanting to ask about boat trips to see marine life.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia! Posso ajudar?
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning! Can I help you?

options.text: Bom dia! Queria saber se há passeios de barco para ver golfinhos.
options.translation: Good morning! I wanted to know if there are boat trips to see dolphins.
options.feedback: Excellent, direct and polite.

options.text: Olá. Têm barcos para o mar?
options.translation: Hello. Do you have boats for the sea?
options.feedback: A bit vague. Try to be more specific about what you want to see.

nodes.npc_text: Barcos para o mar? Sim, temos muitos. Mas para que tipo de passeio?
nodes.npc_translation: Boats for the sea? Yes, we have many. But for what kind of trip?

options.text: Ah, desculpe. Queria saber de passeios para ver a vida marinha.
options.translation: Oh, sorry. I wanted to know about trips to see marine life.
options.feedback: Good recovery, more specific now.

nodes.npc_text: Sim, claro! Temos vários. Há passeios de catamarã e de lancha rápida. Quer ver mais baleias ou golfinhos?
nodes.npc_translation: Yes, of course! We have several. There are catamaran and speedboat trips. Do you want to see more whales or dolphins?

options.text: Gostava de ver golfinhos, se possível. Qual é a melhor altura?
options.translation: I'd like to see dolphins, if possible. What's the best time?
options.feedback: Good choice, showing preference and asking for practical information.

options.text: Tanto faz, o importante é ir para o mar.
options.translation: It doesn't matter, the important thing is to go to sea.
options.feedback: You can be more specific to get better advice. Try asking about a specific animal.

nodes.npc_text: Percebo. Mas se tiver uma preferência, posso dar-lhe melhores sugestões.
nodes.npc_translation: I understand. But if you have a preference, I can give you better suggestions.

options.text: Pois, gostava mais de ver golfinhos. Qual altura do dia é melhor?
options.translation: Right, I'd prefer to see dolphins. What time of day is best?
options.feedback: Well done, you clarified your preference.

nodes.npc_text: Para golfinhos, as manhãs costumam ser melhores, mas à tarde também se vêem. Temos um passeio às dez e outro às duas.
nodes.npc_translation: For dolphins, mornings are usually better, but they are also seen in the afternoon. We have a trip at ten and another at two.

options.text: Ótimo! E qual é a duração do passeio?
options.translation: Great! And what is the duration of the trip?
options.feedback: Good follow-up question, practical for planning.

nodes.npc_text: Os passeios para ver golfinhos duram cerca de duas horas e meia. Inclui uma paragem para nadar, se o tempo permitir.
nodes.npc_translation: Dolphin watching trips last about two and a half hours. It includes a stop for swimming, if the weather allows.

options.text: Muito obrigado pela informação! Vou pensar e já volto.
options.translation: Thank you very much for the information! I'll think about it and come back.
options.feedback: Polite and natural way to end the conversation for now.

Practice asking 'Há barcos que vão ver...?' and 'Qual é a melhor altura p'ra...?'
Think about what you'd like to see (whales or dolphins) and when you'd like to go (manhã/tarde).
fallback_phrases: Pode falar mais devagar, por favor?

goals: Successfully ask if there are boat trips to see marine life (whales/dolphins).
Inquire about the best time of day or year for such trips.
Understand the duration of a typical boat trip.
Engage politely with a local service provider.

sit-d109-planning-porto-santo.title: Planning the Porto Santo Trip
sit-d109-planning-porto-santo.summary: The ferry to the golden island: booking the Lobo Marinho, asking about crossings and the sea state, where to stay, and what Madeirans say about Porto Santo.

phrase_patterns.translation: I wanted to know the {schedules} for {destination}.

variants.text: Estava a pensar nos horários para Porto Santo.
variants.translation: I was thinking about the schedules for Porto Santo.
variants.note: A slightly softer way to ask.

phrase_patterns.base: Como é que o mar está para {dia}?
Onde é que costumas ficar em {local}?
phrase_patterns.translation: Where do you usually stay in {place}?

variants.text: Que sítios é que recomendas para ficar?
variants.translation: What places do you recommend to stay?

vocabulary.note: The name of the ferry boat.
Refers specifically to the long sandy beach of Porto Santo.
A traditional Madeiran alcoholic drink, popular everywhere.

dialogues.title: Porto Santo Trip Chat
dialogues.context: A learner is talking to their neighbour, Maria, about planning a trip to Porto Santo.

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: Olá, Maria! Tudo bem? Estávamos a pensar ir a Porto Santo p'ra semana.
lines.translation: Hi, Maria! All good? We were thinking of going to Porto Santo next week.

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Olá! Tudo ótimo, obrigada. Ah, Porto Santo! É uma boa ideia. Já viram como é que o mar está?
lines.translation: Hi! All great, thanks. Ah, Porto Santo! That's a good idea. Have you already checked how the sea is?

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: Ainda não. É que o Lobo Marinho balança imenso quando o mar está agitado, não é?
lines.translation: Not yet. It's because the Lobo Marinho sways a lot when the sea is rough, doesn't it?

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Pois é. É melhor ver bem a previsão. Mas a ilha é linda, e o areal é um espetáculo! Onde é que pensam ficar?
lines.translation: That's right. It's better to check the forecast well. But the island is beautiful, and the sandy beach is a spectacle! Where are you thinking of staying?

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: Ainda não sabemos. Que sítios é que recomendas? E o que é que costumam fazer lá?
lines.translation: We don't know yet. What places do you recommend? And what do you usually do there?

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Nós gostamos de alugar bicicletas e andar pela ilha. E claro, uma poncha na praia!
lines.translation: We like to rent bicycles and cycle around the island. And of course, a poncha on the beach!

cultural_notes.title: The Lobo Marinho and Sea Sickness
cultural_notes.body: The 'Lobo Marinho' is the only ferry connecting Madeira to Porto Santo. Locals often joke, or complain, about its tendency to sway significantly in rough seas. It's a common topic of conversation when planning a trip, and checking the sea forecast is a practical and culturally relevant step.

cultural_notes.title: Porto Santo: The Golden Island
cultural_notes.body: Porto Santo is known as 'a ilha dourada' (the golden island) due to its long, golden sandy beach (areal). Unlike Madeira's rocky coastline, Porto Santo offers extensive sandy areas. Renting bicycles to explore the island and its beach is a very popular activity among visitors, including Madeirans.

roleplay.scenario: You are at the ferry terminal in Funchal, wanting to ask about ferry times and sea conditions for a trip to Porto Santo. A service assistant approaches you.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia! Posso ajudar o senhor/a senhora?
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning! Can I help you (sir/madam)?

options.text: Bom dia. Queria saber os horários para Porto Santo.
options.translation: Good morning. I wanted to know the schedules for Porto Santo.
options.feedback: A polite and direct request for information.

options.text: Olá. Estou a pensar ir a Porto Santo e queria saber sobre os bilhetes.
options.translation: Hello. I'm thinking of going to Porto Santo and wanted to know about the tickets.
options.feedback: A good alternative, slightly more conversational.

nodes.npc_text: Certo. Para que dia é que estava a pensar?
nodes.npc_translation: Right. For which day were you thinking?

options.text: Para a semana que vem, por favor.
options.translation: For next week, please.
options.feedback: Clearly stating the desired timeframe.

options.text: Ainda não decidimos o dia, mas é para breve.
options.translation: We haven't decided the day yet, but it's for soon.
options.feedback: This is also common, allowing the assistant to give general information.

nodes.npc_text: Ok. Deixe-me ver... Ah, e como é que o mar está, é importante para si?
nodes.npc_translation: Okay. Let me see... Ah, and how the sea is, is that important to you?

options.text: Sim, é muito importante! O Lobo Marinho balança imenso, não é?
options.translation: Yes, it's very important! The Lobo Marinho sways a lot, doesn't it?
options.feedback: Confirming the importance and showing awareness of the ferry's reputation.

options.text: Sim, por favor. Queria saber se o mar vai estar calmo.
options.translation: Yes, please. I wanted to know if the sea will be calm.
options.feedback: A direct and polite way to inquire about sea conditions.

nodes.npc_text: Compreendo. Os horários variam um pouco, mas há sempre travessias diárias. E sobre o mar, é algo que o preocupa?
nodes.npc_translation: I understand. The schedules vary a bit, but there are always daily crossings. And about the sea, is that something that worries you?

options.text: Sim, um bocado. O mar agitado não é bom para mim.
options.translation: Yes, a bit. Rough sea isn't good for me.
options.feedback: Expressing your concern directly.

options.text: Pois, é sempre bom saber se o mar vai estar calmo.
options.translation: Well, it's always good to know if the sea will be calm.
options.feedback: A more general, but still clear, expression of concern.

nodes.npc_text: Pois é, o Lobo Marinho pode balançar. Para a semana, a previsão é de mar calmo. Mas é sempre bom confirmar mais perto da data. Quer que eu veja os preços?
nodes.npc_translation: That's right, the Lobo Marinho can sway. For next week, the forecast is for calm sea. But it's always good to confirm closer to the date. Do you want me to check the prices?

options.text: Sim, por favor. Muito obrigado/a!
options.translation: Yes, please. Thank you very much!
options.feedback: A polite and appreciative response, ending the interaction.

options.text: Não, por agora está bem. Já tenho as informações que queria. Obrigado!
options.translation: No, for now it's fine. I already have the information I wanted. Thanks!
options.feedback: Politely declining further help, showing you achieved your goal.

sit-d110-island-history-stories.title: Island History & Stories
sit-d110-island-history-stories.summary: The stories locals tell: the discovery and the 600 years, the wine story, emigration and return, the 1976 autonomy — following and asking about history told informally.

slots.name: story_element
slots.description: a historical fact or common belief
options: a ilha foi descoberta por acaso

phrase_patterns.base: E como é que era a vida {time_period}?

variants.text: Essa história do {topic} é muito interessante.
variants.translation: That story about {topic} is very interesting.

vocabulary.note: Madeira regionalism

dialogues.title: Talking about island history with a neighbour
dialogues.context: Tiago, a younger resident, is chatting with Vizinha Maria, an older neighbour, on a bench overlooking the sea.

lines.speaker: Tiago
lines.text: Vizinha Maria, sempre ouvi dizer que a ilha foi descoberta por acaso. É verdade?
lines.translation: Neighbour Maria, I always heard that the island was discovered by chance. Is it true?

lines.speaker: Vizinha Maria
lines.text: Pois é, Tiago. Os navegadores portugueses, eles estavam a ir p'ra outro lado e deram com a ilha. Uma sorte, não foi?
lines.translation: That's right, Tiago. The Portuguese navigators, they were going somewhere else and stumbled upon the island. Lucky, wasn't it?

lines.speaker: Tiago
lines.text: Que interessante! E a história do vinho? Quando é que começou a ser tão importante?
lines.translation: How interesting! And the story of the wine? When did it start to be so important?

lines.speaker: Vizinha Maria
lines.text: Ah, o vinho, isso é outra história grande. Desde cedo, desde o século XV, já se exportava. Era a nossa riqueza principal, sabes?
lines.translation: Ah, the wine, that's another big story. From early on, from the 15th century, it was already being exported. It was our main wealth, you know?

lines.speaker: Tiago
lines.text: Imenso tempo! E a autonomia, quando é que isso aconteceu, mesmo? A minha avó fala muito nisso.
lines.translation: A long time! And autonomy, when did that happen, exactly? My grandmother talks about it a lot.

lines.speaker: Vizinha Maria
lines.text: A autonomia foi em 76, Tiago. Mudou muito a ilha, para melhor, claro. Deu-nos mais voz.
lines.translation: Autonomy was in '76, Tiago. It changed the island a lot, for the better, of course. It gave us more of a voice.

cultural_notes.title: Asking about local history
cultural_notes.body: In Madeira, older generations often have a rich oral history of the island, their families, and the local community. Asking about these stories is generally well-received and seen as a sign of respect and genuine interest in local culture. People often enjoy sharing their memories and knowledge.

cultural_notes.title: Common interjections and agreement
cultural_notes.body: You'll often hear 'Pois é' or just 'Pois' used in conversation. It's a common way to express agreement, affirmation, or to acknowledge what someone has said, similar to 'That's right' or 'Indeed' in English. It helps to make your speech sound more natural and engaged.

roleplay.scenario: You are at a small local market stall, buying some fresh produce from a friendly vendor. You want to ask about the history of the products or the area.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia! Quer umas maçãs Reineta? São daqui, da serra.
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning! Would you like some Reineta apples? They're from here, from the mountains.

options.text: Sim, por favor. Sabe, sempre me pergunto como é que estas maçãs vieram parar cá.
options.translation: Yes, please. You know, I always wonder how these apples ended up here.
options.feedback: Good, you're asking about the product's history directly.

options.text: Bom dia! As maçãs parecem ótimas. Esta zona tem muita história, não tem?
options.translation: Good morning! The apples look great. This area has a lot of history, doesn't it?
options.feedback: Excellent, you're opening up a conversation about the local area's past.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, as Reineta... São maçãs antigas, já cá estavam há séculos. Os primeiros colonos trouxeram muitas coisas, sabe?
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, the Reineta... They are old apples, they've been here for centuries. The first settlers brought many things, you know?

options.text: Os primeiros colonos? Que interessante! E a vida deles, como é que era?
options.translation: The first settlers? How interesting! And their life, what was it like?
options.feedback: You're showing interest and asking a follow-up about daily life in the past.

options.text: Pois, faz sentido. E a história da ilha, o que é que acha mais marcante?
options.translation: Right, that makes sense. And the island's history, what do you think is most striking?
options.feedback: You're broadening the conversation to the island's overall history.

nodes.npc_text: Sim, tem imensa história! Aqui perto, as levadas mais antigas foram feitas pelos escravos. É uma história triste, mas faz parte.
nodes.npc_translation: Yes, it has immense history! Nearby, the oldest levadas were made by slaves. It's a sad story, but it's part of it.

options.text: É verdade, é uma parte importante. E a autonomia, mudou muito a vida por aqui?
options.translation: That's true, it's an important part. And autonomy, did it change life here a lot?
options.feedback: You're connecting a specific historical event (autonomy) to local life.

options.text: As levadas são incríveis. Sabe de outras histórias antigas daqui?
options.translation: The levadas are incredible. Do you know other old stories from here?
options.feedback: You're acknowledging the information and asking for more local stories.

nodes.npc_text: Antigamente era tudo mais difícil, claro. As pessoas trabalhavam muito na terra, e muitos tiveram de emigrar p'ra ter uma vida melhor. Conhece alguém que emigrou?
nodes.npc_translation: In the old days, everything was more difficult, of course. People worked a lot on the land, and many had to emigrate to have a better life. Do you know anyone who emigrated?

options.text: Sim, a minha avó falava muito dos tios que foram para a Venezuela.
options.translation: Yes, my grandmother talked a lot about her uncles who went to Venezuela.
options.feedback: You've successfully shared a personal connection to the topic, making the conversation more natural.

options.text: Não, mas ouço muitas histórias. É uma parte importante da nossa história, não é?
options.translation: No, but I hear many stories. It's an important part of our history, isn't it?
options.feedback: You're showing understanding and empathy for the historical context.

nodes.npc_text: Para mim, a autonomia foi um grande passo. Deu-nos mais controlo sobre o nosso futuro. Mas a emigração também marca muito a nossa gente.
nodes.npc_translation: For me, autonomy was a big step. It gave us more control over our future. But emigration also deeply marks our people.

options.text: Concordo. A emigração é uma história de coragem.
options.translation: I agree. Emigration is a story of courage.
options.feedback: You're expressing agreement and a thoughtful opinion, maintaining the flow of conversation.

options.text: Pois, e agora muitos estão a voltar, não é?
options.translation: Right, and now many are returning, aren't they?
options.feedback: You're linking the past to the present, showing a broader understanding of the topic.

likely_responses: Ah, isso já foi há muito tempo...
